    Self::InvalidIndentIncrease => \"E004\",\n75\t            Self::TabCharacter => \"E005\",\n76\t        }\n77\t    }\n78\t\n79\t    /// Get all indentation error types\n80\t    pub fn all() -> &'static [Self] {\n81\t        &[\n82\t            Self::MixedTabsAndSpaces,\n83\t            Self::InvalidIndentLevel,\n84\t            Self::ExcessiveIndentIncrease,\n85\t            Self::InvalidIndentIncrease,\n86\t            Self::TabCharacter,\n87\t        ]\n88\t    }\n89\t}\n90\t\n91\timpl fmt::Display for IndentationErrorType {\n92\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n93\t        write!(f, \"{}: {}\", self.code(), self.description())\n94\t    }\n95\t}\n96\t\n97\t/// Classification of delimiter error types\n98\t///\n99\t/// This enum provides structured categorization of different delimiter\n100\t/// error types that can occur in YAML files.\n101\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\n102\tpub enum DelimiterErrorType {\n103\t    /// Missing colon after a key\n104\t    MissingColon,\n105\t    /// Unmatched opening bracket '['\n106\t    UnmatchedOpeningBracket,\n107\t    /// Unmatched closing bracket ']'\n108\t    UnmatchedClosingBracket,\n109\t    /// Unclosed bracket '['\n110\t    UnclosedBracket,\n111\t    /// Unmatched opening brace '{'\n112\t    UnmatchedOpeningBrace,\n113\t    /// Unmatched closing brace '}'\n114\t    UnmatchedClosingBrace,\n115\t    /// Unclosed brace '{'\n116\t    UnclosedBrace,\n117\t    /// Mismatched quotes (single inside double or vice versa)\n118\t    MismatchedQuotes,\n119\t    /// Unclosed single quote\n120\t    UnclosedSingleQuote,\n121\t    /// Unclosed double quote\n122\t    UnclosedDoubleQuote,\n123\t}\n124\t\n125\timpl DelimiterErrorType {\n126\t    /// Get a human-readable description of this error type\n127\t    pub fn description(&self) -> &'static str {\n128\t        match self {\n129\t            Self::MissingColon => \"missing colon after key\",\n130\t            Self::UnmatchedOpeningBracket => \"unmatched opening bracket '['\",\n131\t            Self::UnmatchedClosingBracket => \"unmatched closing bracket ']'\",\n132\t            Self::UnclosedBracket => \"unclosed bracket '['\",\n133\t            Self::UnmatchedOpeningBrace => \"unmatched opening brace '{'\",\n134\t            Self::UnmatchedClosingBrace => \"unmatched closing brace '}'\",\n135\t            Self::UnclosedBrace => \"unclosed brace '{'\",\n136\t            Self::MismatchedQuotes => \"mismatched quotes\",\n137\t            Self::UnclosedSingleQuote => \"unclosed single quote\",\n138\t            Self::UnclosedDoubleQuote => \"unclosed double quote\",\n139\t        }\n140\t    }\n141\t\n142\t    /// Get a short code for this error type\n143\t    pub fn code(&self) -> &'static str {\n144\t        match self {\n145\t            Self::MissingColon => \"D001\",\n146\t            Self::UnmatchedOpeningBracket => \"D002\",\n147\t            Self::UnmatchedClosingBracket => \"D003\",\n148\t            Self::UnclosedBracket => \"D004\",\n149\t            Self::UnmatchedOpeningBrace => \"D005\",\n150\t            Self::UnmatchedClosingBrace => \"D006\",\n151\t            Self::UnclosedBrace => \"D007\",\n152\t            Self::MismatchedQuotes => \"D008\",\n153\t            Self::UnclosedSingleQuote => \"D009\",\n154\t            Self::UnclosedDoubleQuote => \"D010\",\n155\t        }\n156\t    }\n157\t\n158\t    /// Get all delimiter error types\n159\t    pub fn all() -> &'static [Self] {\n160\t        &[\n161\t            Self::MissingColon,\n162\t            Self::UnmatchedOpeningBracket,\n163\t            Self::UnmatchedClosingBracket,\n164\t            Self::UnclosedBracket,\n165\t            Self::UnmatchedOpeningBrace,\n166\t            Self::UnmatchedClosingBrace,\n167\t            Self::UnclosedBrace,\n168\t            Self::MismatchedQuotes,\n169\t            Self::UnclosedSingleQuote,\n170\t            Self::UnclosedDoubleQuote,\n171\t        ]\n172\t    }\n173\t}\n174\t\n175\timpl fmt::Display for DelimiterErrorType {\n176\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n177\t        write!(f, \"{}: {}\", self.code(), self.description())\n178\t    }\n179\t}\n180\t\n181\t/// Main syntax detector for YAML content\n182\t///\n183\t/// This struct orchestrates all syntax detection checks and provides\n184\t/// a unified interface for error detection.\n185\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n186\tpub struct SyntaxDetector {\n187\t    /// Configuration for detection behavior\n188\t    config: DetectorConfig,\n189\t    /// Track indentation state across lines\n190\t    indentation_state: IndentationState,\n191\t    /// Track delimiter state (brackets, braces, quotes)\n192\t    delimiter_state: DelimiterState,\n193\t    /// Track structure state (keys, sequences, mappings)\n194\t    structure_state: StructureState,\n195\t}\n196\t\n197\t/// Configuration for syntax detection behavior\n198\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n199\tpub struct DetectorConfig {\n200\t    /// Whether to detect tab/space mixing\n201\t    pub detect_mixed_indentation: bool,\n202\t    /// Whether to check for consistent indentation (multiples of base)\n203\t    pub check_consistent_indentation: bool,\n204\t    /// Base indentation size (usually 2 or 4)\n205\t    pub base_indent_size: usize,\n206\t    /// Whether to validate delimiter balancing\n207\t    pub validate_delimiter_balance: bool,\n208\t    /// Whether to detect duplicate keys\n209\t    pub detect_duplicate_keys: bool,\n210\t    /// Whether to detect invalid sequence syntax\n211\t    pub detect_invalid_sequences: bool,\n212\t    /// Whether to detect invalid mapping syntax\n213\t    pub detect_invalid_mappings: bool,\n214\t}\n215\t\n216\timpl Default for DetectorConfig {\n217\t    fn default() -> Self {\n218\t        Self {\n219\t            detect_mixed_indentation: true,\n220\t            check_consistent_indentation: true,\n221\t            base_indent_size: 2,\n222\t            validate_delimiter_balance: true,\n223\t            detect_duplicate_keys: true,\n224\t            detect_invalid_sequences: true,\n225\t            detect_invalid_mappings: true,\n226\t        }\n227\t    }\n228\t}\n229\t\n230\t/// State tracking for indentation analysis\n231\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\n232\tstruct IndentationState {\n233\t    /// Whether tabs have been seen in the file\n234\t    has_tabs: bool,\n235\t    /// Whether spaces have been seen in the file\n236\t    has_spaces: bool,\n237\t    /// Previous line's indentation level (in spaces)\n238\t    prev_indent_level: usize,\n239\t    /// Expected indentation levels for nested contexts\n240\t    indent_stack: Vec<usize>,\n241\t    /// Lines with mixed indentation\n242\t    mixed_lines: Vec<usize>,\n243\t}\n244\t\n245\t/// State tracking for delimiter analysis\n246\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\n247\tstruct DelimiterState {\n248\t    /// Stack of opening brackets/braces\n249\t    bracket_stack: Vec<(char, usize)>, // (bracket_type, line_number)\n250\t    /// Current quote state (None, Single, Double)\n251\t    quote_state: Option<QuoteType>,\n252\t    /// Lines with quote errors\n253\t    quote_errors: Vec<usize>,\n254\t    /// Whether we're inside a multiline block (|, >)\n255\t    in_multiline_block: bool,\n256\t    /// Indentation level of the multiline block start\n257\t    multiline_block_indent: usize,\n258\t    /// Whether we're inside flow-style context (within [] or {})\n259\t    in_flow_context: bool,\n260\t}\n261\t\n262\t/// Quote type for tracking\n263\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\n264\tenum QuoteType {\n265\t    Single,\n266\t    Double,\n267\t}\n268\t\n269\t/// A scope representing a mapping context at a specific nesting level\n270\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n271\tstruct Scope {\n272\t    /// Indentation level (number of leading spaces)\n273\t    indent_level: usize,\n274\t    /// Keys defined within this scope\n275\t    keys: HashSet<String>,\n276\t    /// Line number where this scope started (for error reporting)\n277\t    start_line: usize,\n278\t    /// Parent key that created this scope (e.g., \"web\" in \"services: {...}\")\n279\t    parent_key: Option<String>,\n280\t    /// Whether this scope is in flow-style mapping ({key: value})\n281\t    is_flow_style: bool,\n282\t    /// Whether this scope is within a sequence context\n283\t    in_sequence_context: bool,\n284\t    /// Unique identifier for sequence items to distinguish them at same indent\n285\t    sequence_item_id: Option<usize>,\n286\t}\n287\t\n288\timpl Scope {\n289\t    /// Create a new scope\n290\t    fn new(indent_level: usize, start_line: usize, parent_key: Option<String>) -> Self {\n291\t        Self {\n292\t            indent_level,\n293\t            keys: HashSet::new(),\n294\t            start_line,\n295\t            parent_key,\n296\t            is_flow_style: false,\n297\t            in_sequence_context: false,\n298\t            sequence_item_id: None,\n299\t        }\n300\t    }\n301\t\n302\t    /// Add a key to this scope, returning true if it's a duplicate\n303\t    fn add_key(&mut self, key: &str) -> bool {\n304\t        !self.keys.insert(key.to_string())\n305\t    }\n306\t\n307\t    /// Check if this scope contains a key\n308\t    fn contains_key(&self, key: &str) -> bool {\n309\t        self.keys.contains(key)\n310\t    }\n311\t\n312\t    /// Check if this scope is equivalent to another for duplicate key purposes\n313\t    /// (same indent level and same sequence item)\n314\t    fn is_same_scope_as(&self, other: &Scope) -> bool {\n315\t        self.indent_level == other.indent_level\n316\t            && self.in_sequence_context == other.in_sequence_context\n317\t            && self.sequence_item_id == other.sequence_item_id\n318\t    }\n319\t}\n320\t\n321\t/// Hierarchical stack of active scopes\n322\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n323\tstruct ScopeStack {\n324\t    /// Stack of active scopes (top = current scope)\n325\t    scopes: Vec<Scope>,\n326\t    /// Base indentation size (usually 2 or 4 spaces)\n327\t    base_indent: usize,\n328\t    /// Sequence item counter for generating unique IDs\n329\t    sequence_item_counter: usize,\n330\t}\n331\t\n332\timpl ScopeStack {\n333\t    /// Create a new scope stack\n334\t    fn new(base_indent: usize) -> Self {\n335\t        Self {\n336\t            scopes: vec![Scope::new(0, 0, None)], // Root scope\n337\t            base_indent,\n338\t            sequence_item_counter: 0,\n339\t        }\n340\t    }\n341\t\n342\t    /// Get the current scope (top of stack)\n343\t    fn current_scope(&mut self) -> &mut Scope {\n344\t        self.scopes.last_mut().expect(\"Scope stack should never be empty\")\n345\t    }\n346\t\n347\t    /// Get scope for a specific indentation level\n348\t    fn get_scope_at_level(&self, indent_level: usize) -> Option<&Scope> {\n349\t        self.scopes.iter().find(|s| s.indent_level == indent_level)\n350\t    }\n351\t\n352\t    /// Enter a new scope (when indent increases)\n353\t    fn enter_scope(&mut self, indent_level: usize, line: usize, parent_key: Option<String>) {\n354\t        // Remove all scopes deeper than this level (fresh start for sibling mappings)\n355\t        self.scopes.retain(|s| s.indent_level < indent_level);\n356\t\n357\t        // Create a fresh scope at this level\n358\t        let new_scope = Scope::new(indent_level, line, parent_key);\n359\t        self.scopes.push(new_scope);\n360\t    }\n361\t\n362\t    /// Exit to parent scope (when indent decreases)\n363\t    fn exit_to_scope(&mut self, target_indent: usize) {\n364\t        // Remove all scopes deeper than target\n365\t        self.scopes.retain(|s| s.indent_level <= target_indent);\n366\t    }\n367\t\n368\t    /// Check if current scope contains a key\n369\t    fn contains_key(&self, key: &str) -> bool {\n370\t        self.scopes.last()\n371\t            .map(|scope| scope.contains_key(key))\n372\t            .unwrap_or(false)\n373\t    }\n374\t\n375\t    /// Add a key to current scope\n376\t    fn add_key(&mut self, key: &str) {\n377\t        let scope = self.current_scope();\n378\t        scope.add_key(key);\n379\t    }\n380\t\n381\t    /// Get human-readable path to current scope\n382\t    fn get_scope_path(&self) -> String {\n383\t        let mut path = Vec::new();\n384\t        for scope in &self.scopes {\n385\t            if let Some(ref key) = scope.parent_key {\n386\t                path.push(key.clone());\n387\t            }\n388\t        }\n389\t        path.join(\".\")\n390\t    }\n391\t\n392\t    /// Get current indent level\n393\t    fn current_indent(&self) -> usize {\n394\t        self.scopes.last()\n395\t            .map(|scope| scope.indent_level)\n396\t            .unwrap_or(0)\n397\t    }\n398\t\n399\t    /// Enter a sequence context (when we see a `-` item)\n400\t    fn enter_sequence_scope(&mut self, indent_level: usize, line: usize) {\n401\t        // Remove all scopes deeper than this level\n402\t        self.scopes.retain(|s| s.indent_level < indent_level);\n403\t\n404\t        // Check if there's already a scope at this level that's in a sequence context\n405\t        let needs_new_scope = self.scopes.last()\n406\t            .map(|scope| scope.indent_level != indent_level || !scope.in_sequence_context)\n407\t            .unwrap_or(true);\n408\t\n409\t        if needs_new_scope {\n410\t            // Create a new scope for this sequence item\n411\t            let mut new_scope = Scope::new(indent_level, line, None);\n412\t            new_scope.in_sequence_context = true;\n413\t            self.sequence_item_counter += 1;\n414\t            new_scope.sequence_item_id = Some(self.sequence_item_counter);\n415\t            self.scopes.push(new_scope);\n416\t        } else {\n417\t            // Reset the existing scope for a new sequence item\n418\t            if let Some(scope) = self.scopes.last_mut() {\n419\t                scope.keys.clear();\n420\t                scope.start_line = line;\n421\t                self.sequence_item_counter += 1;\n422\t                scope.sequence_item_id = Some(self.sequence_item_counter);\n423\t            }\n424\t        }\n425\t    }\n426\t\n427\t    /// Check if we're in a sequence context\n428\t    fn in_sequence_context(&self) -> bool {\n429\t        self.scopes.last()\n430\t            .map(|scope| scope.in_sequence_context)\n431\t            .unwrap_or(false)\n432\t    }\n433\t}\n434\t\n435\timpl Default for ScopeStack {\n436\t    fn default() -> Self {\n437\t        Self::new(2) // Default base indent of 2 spaces\n438\t    }\n439\t}\n440\t\n441\t/// State tracking for structure analysis\n442\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\n443\tstruct StructureState {\n444\t    /// Stack of nested structures (mapping, sequence, etc.)\n445\t    context_stack: Vec<StructureContext>,\n446\t    /// Hierarchical scope-based key tracking\n447\t    scope_stack: ScopeStack,\n448\t    /// Previous line's indentation level\n449\t    prev_indent: usize,\n450\t}\n451\t\n452\t/// Current structure context\n453\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\n454\tenum StructureContext {\n455\t    Mapping,\n456\t    Sequence,\n457\t    FlowMapping,\n458\t    FlowSequence,\n459\t}\n460\t\n461\timpl SyntaxDetector {\n462\t    /// Create a new syntax detector with default configuration\n463\t    pub fn new() -> Self {\n464\t        Self::with_config(DetectorConfig::default())\n465\t    }\n466\t\n467\t    /// Create a new syntax detector with custom configuration\n468\t    pub fn with_config(config: DetectorConfig) -> Self {\n469\t        Self {\n470\t            config,\n471\t            indentation_state: IndentationState::default(),\n472\t            delimiter_state: DelimiterState::default(),\n473\t            structure_state: StructureState::default(),\n474\t        }\n475\t    }\n476\t\n477\t    /// Detect all syntax errors in YAML content\n478\t    ///\n479\t    /// This is the main entry point for syntax detection. It runs all\n480\t    /// enabled detectors and returns a comprehensive list of errors.\n481\t    ///\n482\t    /// # Arguments\n483\t    /// * `content` - The YAML content to analyze\n484\t    ///\n485\t    /// # Returns\n486\t    /// A vector of validation errors representing all detected syntax issues\n487\t    pub fn detect_errors(&mut self, content: &str) -> Vec<ValidationError> {\n488\t        let mut errors = Vec::new();\n489\t        let lines: Vec<&str> = content.lines().collect();\n490\t\n491\t        // Reset state for new detection\n492\t        self.reset_state();\n493\t\n494\t        for (line_num, line) in lines.iter().enumerate() {\n495\t            let line_num_1index = line_num + 1; // Convert to 1-indexed\n496\t\n497\t            // Skip empty lines and comments for most checks\n498\t            let is_meaningful = !line.trim().is_empty() && !line.trim().starts_with('#');\n499\t\n500\t            if is_meaningful {\n501\t                // Run all enabled detectors\n502\t                if self.config.detect_mixed_indentation || self.config.check_consistent_indentation {\n503\t                    self.detect_indentation_errors(line, line_num_1index, &mut errors);\n504\t                }\n505\t\n506\t                if self.config.validate_delimiter_balance {\n507\t                    self.detect_delimiter_errors(line, line_num_1index, &mut errors);\n508\t                }\n509\t\n510\t                if self.config.detect_invalid_sequences || self.config.detect_invalid_mappings {\n511\t                    self.detect_structure_errors(line, line_num_1index, &mut errors);\n512\t                }\n513\t\n514\t                if self.config.detect_duplicate_keys {\n515\t                    self.detect_duplicate_key_errors(line, line_num_1index, &mut errors);\n516\t                }\n517\t            }\n518\t        }\n519\t\n520\t        // Final validation checks\n521\t        self.finalize_delimiter_checks(&mut errors);\n522\t        self.finalize_structure_checks(&mut errors);\n523\t\n524\t        errors\n525\t    }\n526\t\n527\t    /// Detect syntax errors and convert to ValidationResult\n528\t    pub fn detect_to_validation_result(&mut self, content: &str) -> ValidationResult {\n529\t        let errors = self.detect_errors(content);\n530\t\n531\t        if errors.is_empty() {\n532\t            ValidationResult::success()\n533\t        } else {\n534\t            ValidationResult::failure(errors)\n535\t        }\n536\t    }\n537\t\n538\t    /// Reset all state tracking for a new detection run\n539\t    fn reset_state(&mut self) {\n540\t        self.indentation_state = IndentationState::default();\n541\t        self.delimiter_state = DelimiterState::default();\n542\t        self.structure_state = StructureState::default();\n543\t    }\n544\t\n545\t    /// Detect indentation-related errors\n546\t    fn detect_indentation_errors(&mut self, line: &str, line_num: usize, errors: &mut Vec<ValidationError>) {\n547\t        let leading_whitespace_len = self.get_leading_whitespace_length(line);\n548\t\n549\t        // Check for mixed tabs and spaces in leading whitespace\n550\t        if self.config.detect_mixed_indentation && leading_whitespace_len > 0 {\n551\t            let leading_chars: Vec<char> = line.chars().take(leading_whitespace_len).collect();\n552\t            let has_tabs = leading_chars.contains(&'\\t');\n553\t            let has_spaces = leading_chars.contains(&' ');\n554\t\n555\t            if has_tabs && has_spaces {\n556\t                self.indentation_state.has_tabs = true;\n557\t                self.indentation_state.has_spaces = true;\n558\t                self.indentation_state.mixed_lines.push(line_num);\n559\t\n560\t                let error_type = IndentationErrorType::MixedTabsAndSpaces;\n561\t                errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n562\t                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n563\t                    error_type.description()\n564\t                ).with_line(line_num).with_indentation_error_type(error_type));\n565\t            } else if has_tabs {\n566\t                self.indentation_state.has_tabs = true;\n567\t                // Optionally report tab-only indentation\n568\t                if self.config.detect_mixed_indentation {\n569\t                    let error_type = IndentationErrorType::TabCharacter;\n570\t                    errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n571\t                        format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n572\t                        format!(\"{}: {}\", error_type.code(), error_type.description())\n573\t                    ).with_line(line_num).with_indentation_error_type(error_type));\n574\t                }\n575\t            } else if has_spaces {\n576\t                self.indentation_state.has_spaces = true;\n577\t            }\n578\t        }\n579\t\n580\t        // Check for consistent indentation levels\n581\t        if self.config.check_consistent_indentation && leading_whitespace_len > 0 {\n582\t            let indent_level = leading_whitespace_len;\n583\t\n584\t            // Check if indentation is a multiple of base_indent_size\n585\t            if indent_level % self.config.base_indent_size != 0 {\n586\t                let error_type = IndentationErrorType::InvalidIndentLevel;\n587\t                errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n588\t                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n589\t                    format!(\"{}: {} spaces is not a multiple of {}\",\n590\t                            error_type.code(), indent_level, self.config.base_indent_size)\n591\t                ).with_line(line_num).with_indentation_error_type(error_type));\n592\t            }\n593\t\n594\t            // Check if indentation increase is consistent\n595\t            if indent_level > self.indentation_state.prev_indent_level {\n596\t                let increase = indent_level - self.indentation_state.prev_indent_level;\n597\t\n598\t                // Check if increase is too large (more than 2 levels at once)\n599\t                if increase > self.config.base_indent_size * 2 {\n600\t                    let error_type = IndentationErrorType::ExcessiveIndentIncrease;\n601\t                    errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n602\t                        format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n603\t                        format!(\"{}: increase of {} spaces exceeds maximum of {} levels\",\n604\t                                error_type.code(), increase, self.config.base_indent_size * 2)\n605\t                    ).with_line(line_num).with_indentation_error_type(error_type));\n606\t                }\n607\t\n608\t                if increase % self.config.base_indent_size != 0 {\n609\t                    let error_type = IndentationErrorType::InvalidIndentIncrease;\n610\t                    errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n611\t                        format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n612\t                        format!(\"{}: increase of {} spaces is not a multiple of {}\",\n613\t                                error_type.code(), increase, self.config.base_indent_size)\n614\t                    ).with_line(line_num).with_indentation_error_type(error_type));\n615\t                } else {\n616\t                    // Valid increase - push to stack\n617\t                    self.indentation_state.indent_stack.push(indent_level);\n618\t                }\n619\t            } else if indent_level < self.indentation_state.prev_indent_level {\n620\t                // Decrease in indentation - pop from stack\n621\t                while !self.indentation_state.indent_stack.is_empty()\n622\t                      && *self.indentation_state.indent_stack.last().unwrap() > indent_level {\n623\t                    self.indentation_state.indent_stack.pop();\n624\t                }\n625\t            }\n626\t\n627\t            self.indentation_state.prev_indent_level = indent_level;\n628\t        }\n629\t    }\n630\t\n631\t    /// Detect delimiter-related errors\n632\t    fn detect_delimiter_errors(&mut self, line: &str, line_num: usize, errors: &mut Vec<ValidationError>) {\n633\t        let leading_whitespace_len = self.get_leading_whitespace_length(line);\n634\t        let trimmed = line.trim();\n635\t\n636\t        // Check if we're inside a multiline block\n637\t        if self.delimiter_state.in_multiline_block {\n638\t            // Multiline blocks continue until we find a line with less or equal indentation\n639\t            // that's not empty or a comment\n640\t            if leading_whitespace_len > self.delimiter_state.multiline_block_indent ||\n641\t               (leading_whitespace_len == self.delimiter_state.multiline_block_indent && (trimmed.is_empty() || trimmed.starts_with('#'))) {\n642\t                return; // Skip lines inside the multiline block\n643\t            } else {\n644\t                // Exit multiline block when indentation decreases\n645\t                self.delimiter_state.in_multiline_block = false;\n646\t                self.delimiter_state.multiline_block_indent = 0;\n647\t            }\n648\t        }\n649\t\n650\t        // Skip empty lines and comments (but not when inside multiline blocks - handled above)\n651\t        if trimmed.is_empty() || trimmed.starts_with('#') {\n652\t            return;\n653\t        }\n654\t\n655\t        // Handle document markers (---, ...)\n656\t        if trimmed == \"---\" || trimmed == \"...\" {\n657\t            return; // Document markers should not be flagged for missing colons\n658\t        }\n659\t\n660\t        // Handle multiline block scalars (|, >, |-, |- , >-, >+, |+)\n661\t        // These appear after a colon, like \"key: |\" or \"key: >\"\n662\t        if trimmed.contains(\": |\") || trimmed.contains(\": >\") ||\n663\t           trimmed.contains(\":|-\") || trimmed.contains(\":>-\") ||\n664\t           trimmed.contains(\":|+\") || trimmed.contains(\":>+\") {\n665\t            self.delimiter_state.in_multiline_block = true;\n666\t            self.delimiter_state.multiline_block_indent = leading_whitespace_len; // Content must be MORE indented than this line\n667\t        }\n668\t\n669\t        // Check for missing colons after keys\n670\t        if !trimmed.starts_with('-') && // Not a sequence item\n671\t           !trimmed.starts_with('?') && // Not an explicit key\n672\t           !trimmed.starts_with(':') && // Not a value\n673\t           !trimmed.starts_with('&') && // Not an anchor\n674\t           !trimmed.starts_with('*') && // Not an alias\n675\t           !trimmed.starts_with('!') && // Not a tag\n676\t           !trimmed.starts_with('|') && // Not a literal block scalar\n677\t           !trimmed.starts_with('>') && // Not a folded block scalar\n678\t           !trimmed.contains('{') && // Not flow style mapping\n679\t           !trimmed.contains('[') && // Not flow style sequence\n680\t           !trimmed.contains(':') && // No colon at all\n681\t           !trimmed.is_empty() &&\n682\t           self.looks_like_key(trimmed) {\n683\t\n684\t            let error_type = DelimiterErrorType::MissingColon;\n685\t            errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n686\t                format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n687\t                format!(\"{}: {}\", error_type.code(), error_type.description())\n688\t            ).with_line(line_num).with_delimiter_error_type(error_type));\n689\t        }\n690\t\n691\t        // Track bracket/brace balance and flow context\n692\t        for (char_pos, ch) in line.chars().enumerate() {\n693\t            match ch {\n694\t                '[' => {\n695\t                    self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack.push(('[', line_num));\n696\t                    self.delimiter_state.in_flow_context = true;\n697\t                }\n698\t                ']' => {\n699\t                    if let Some(('[', _)) = self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack.last() {\n700\t                        self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack.pop();\n701\t                        // Update flow context when stack becomes empty\n702\t                        self.delimiter_state.in_flow_context = !self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack.is_empty();\n703\t                    } else {\n704\t                        let error_type = DelimiterErrorType::UnmatchedClosingBracket;\n705\t                        errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n706\t                            format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n707\t                            format!(\"{}: {}\", error_type.code(), error_type.description())\n708\t                        ).with_line(line_num).with_delimiter_error_type(error_type));\n709\t                    }\n710\t                }\n711\t                '{' => {\n712\t                    self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack.push(('{', line_num));\n713\t                    self.delimiter_state.in_flow_context = true;\n714\t                }\n715\t                '}' => {\n716\t                    if let Some(('{', _)) = self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack.last() {\n717\t                        self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack.pop();\n718\t                        // Update flow context when stack becomes empty\n719\t                        self.delimiter_state.in_flow_context = !self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack.is_empty();\n720\t                    } else {\n721\t                        let error_type = DelimiterErrorType::UnmatchedClosingBrace;\n722\t                        errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n723\t                            format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n724\t                            format!(\"{}: {}\", error_type.code(), error_type.description())\n725\t                        ).with_line(line_num).with_delimiter_error_type(error_type));\n726\t                    }\n727\t                }\n728\t                '\\'' => {\n729\t                    match self.delimiter_state.quote_state {\n730\t                        None => self.delimiter_state.quote_state = Some(QuoteType::Single),\n731\t                        Some(QuoteType::Single) => self.delimiter_state.quote_state = None,\n732\t                        Some(QuoteType::Double) => {\n733\t                            // Mismatched quote\n734\t                            let error_type = DelimiterErrorType::MismatchedQuotes;\n735\t                            errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n736\t                                format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n737\t                                format!(\"{}: {} (single inside double)\", error_type.code(), error_type.description())\n738\t                            ).with_line(line_num).with_delimiter_error_type(error_type));\n739\t                            self.delimiter_state.quote_errors.push(line_num);\n740\t                        }\n741\t                    }\n742\t                }\n743\t                '\"' => {\n744\t                    match self.delimiter_state.quote_state {\n745\t                        None => self.delimiter_state.quote_state = Some(QuoteType::Double),\n746\t                        Some(QuoteType::Double) => self.delimiter_state.quote_state = None,\n747\t                        Some(QuoteType::Single) => {\n748\t                            // Mismatched quote\n749\t                            let error_type = DelimiterErrorType::MismatchedQuotes;\n750\t                            errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n751\t                                format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n752\t                                format!(\"{}: {} (double inside single)\", error_type.code(), error_type.description())\n753\t                            ).with_line(line_num).with_delimiter_error_type(error_type));\n754\t                            self.delimiter_state.quote_errors.push(line_num);\n755\t                        }\n756\t                    }\n757\t                }\n758\t                _ => {}\n759\t            }\n760\t        }\n761\t    }\n762\t\n763\t    /// Detect structure-related errors\n764\t    fn detect_structure_errors(&mut self, line: &str, line_num: usize, errors: &mut Vec<ValidationError>) {\n765\t        let trimmed = line.trim();\n766\t\n767\t        // Detect invalid sequence syntax\n768\t        if self.config.detect_invalid_sequences {\n769\t            if trimmed.starts_with(\"- \") {\n770\t                // Valid sequence item\n771\t                if self.structure_state.context_stack.is_empty() ||\n772\t                   !matches!(self.structure_state.context_stack.last(), Some(StructureContext::Sequence)) {\n773\t                    self.structure_state.context_stack.push(StructureContext::Sequence);\n774\t                }\n775\t            } else if trimmed.starts_with('-') && trimmed.len() > 1 {\n776\t                // Dash followed by something other than space\n777\t                if let Some(next_char) = trimmed.chars().nth(1) {\n778\t                    if !next_char.is_whitespace() {\n779\t                        errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n780\t                            format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n781\t                            \"sequence dash '-' must be followed by whitespace\"\n782\t                        ).with_line(line_num));\n783\t                    }\n784\t                }\n785\t            }\n786\t        }\n787\t\n788\t        // Detect invalid mapping syntax\n789\t        if self.config.detect_invalid_mappings {\n790\t            if let Some(colon_pos) = trimmed.find(':') {\n791\t                let before_colon = &trimmed[..colon_pos];\n792\t\n793\t                // Check if colon is properly used after a key\n794\t                if !before_colon.is_empty() && !before_colon.ends_with(' ') {\n795\t                    // Valid key: value pair\n796\t                    if self.structure_state.context_stack.is_empty() ||\n797\t                       !matches!(self.structure_state.context_stack.last(), Some(StructureContext::Mapping)) {\n798\t                        self.structure_state.context_stack.push(StructureContext::Mapping);\n799\t                    }\n800\t                } else if before_colon.is_empty() {\n801\t                    // Colon at start      format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n805\t                            \"colon ':' at start must be followed by space\"\n806\t                        ).with_line(line_num));\n807\t   self.structure_state.context_stack.push(if trimmed.contains('{') {\n815\t                StructureContext::FlowMapping\n816\t            } else {\n817\t                StructureContext::FlowSequence\n818\t            });\n819\t        }\n820\t    }\n821\t\n822\t    /// Detect duplicate key errors using scope-aware tracking\n823\t    fn detect_duplicate_key_errors(&mut self, line: &str, line_num: usize, errors: &mut Vec<ValidationError>) {\n824\t        // Skip duplicate key detection when inside flow-style contexts ([] or {})\n825\t        // Flow-style YAML uses {key: value} syntax which should not be treated as duplicate keys\n826\t        if self.delimiter_state.in_flow_context {\n827\t            return;\n828\t        }\n829\t\n830\t        let trimmed = line.trim();\n831\t        let indent = self.get_leading_whitespace_length(line);\n832\t\n833\t        // Check if this is a sequence item with a key-value pair (e.g., \"- key: value\")\n834\t        // This creates a new scope for each sequence item\n835\t        if trimmed.starts_with(\"- \") {\n836\t            if let Some(colon_pos) = trimmed.find(':') {\n837\t                // We have \"- key: value\" format\n838\t                let key_part = &trimmed[2..colon_pos]; // Skip \"- \" to get the key\n839\t                let key = key_part.trim();\n840\t\n841\t                // Only enter sequence scope if it looks like a valid mapping key\n842\t                if !key.is_empty() && !key.starts_with('\\'') && !key.starts_with('\"')\n843\t                    && !key.contains('#') && !key.contains('?') {\n844\t                    self.structure_state.scope_stack.enter_sequence_scope(indent, line_num);\n845\t                }\n846\t            }\n847\t            // Sequence items don't need duplicate checking at this level\n848\t            // The keys within the sequence item will be checked when we process them\n849\t            return;\n850\t        }\n851\t\n852\t        // Extract key if this is a key-value pair\n853\t        if let Some(colon_pos) = trimmed.find(':') {\n854\t            let key_part = &trimmed[..colon_pos];\n855\t\n856\t            // Check if this is a parent key (ends with colon and no value on same line)\n857\t            // A parent key typically has nothing after the colon or just whitespace/comments\n858\t            let after_colon = trimmed[colon_pos + 1..].trim();\n859\t            let is_parent_key = after_colon.is_empty() ||\n860\t                                after_colon.starts_with('#');\n861\t\n862\t            if is_parent_key {\n863\t                // This is a parent key - extract the key name and enter a new scope\n864\t                if !key.starts_with('\"') && !key.contains('#') {\n867\t                        // Enter a new scope for the parent key's nested content\n868\t                        self.structure_state.scope_stack.enter_scope(\n869\t                            indent + self.config.base_indent_size,\n870\t                            line_num,\n871\t                            Some(key.to_string())\n872\t                        );\n873\t                    }\n874\t                }\n875\t                return;\n876\t            }\n877\t\n878\t            // Skip if it's a value or not a simple key\n879\t            if key_part.is_empty() || key_part.contains('-') || key_part.contains('?') {\n880\t                return;\n881\t            }\n882\t\n883\t            let key = key_part.trim();\n884\t\n885\t            // Skip quoted keys and special keys\n886\t            if key.starts_with('\\'') || key.starts_with('\"') || key.contains('#') {\n887\t                return;\n888\t            }\n889\t\n890\t            // Handle scope transitions based on indentation changes\n891\t            use std::cmp::Ordering;\n892\t            match indent.cmp(&self.structure_state.scope_stack.current_indent()) {\n893\t                Ordering::Greater => {\n894\t                    // Indent increased - this should be a parent key (handled above)\n895\t                    // If we get here, it's a regular key at deeper indent without a parent\n896\t                    // Enter an anonymous scope\n897\t                    self.structure_state.scope_stack.enter_scope(indent, line_num, None);\n898\t                }\n899\t                Ordering::Less => {\n900\t                    // Indent decreased - exit to the appropriate parent scope\n901\t                    self.structure_state.scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n902\t                }\n903\t                Ordering::Equal => {\n904\t                    // Same scope level - continue checking for duplicates\n905\t                }\n906\t            }\n907\t\n908\t            // Check for duplicates in the current scope\n909\t            if self.structure_state.scope_stack.contains_key(key) {\n910\t                let scope_path = self.structure_state.scope_stack.get_scope_path();\n911\t                let error_message = if scope_path.is_empty() {\n912\t                    format!(\"duplicate key '{}' in mapping scope\", key)\n913\t                } else {\n914\t                    format!(\"duplicate key '{}' in mapping scope '{}'\", key, scope_path)\n915\t                };\n916\t                errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n917\t                    format!(\"key_{}\", key),\n918\t                    error_message\n919\t                ).with_line(line_num));\n920\t            } else {\n921\t                self.structure_state.scope_stack.add_key(key);\n922\t            }\n923\t        }\n924\t\n925\t        // Update previous indentation for next comparison\n926\t        self.structure_state.prev_indent = indent;\n927\t    }\n928\t\n929\t    /// Finalize delimiter checks after processing all lines\n930\t    fn finalize_delimiter_checks(&mut self, errors: &mut Vec<ValidationError>) {\n931\t        // Check for unclosed brackets\n932\t        for (bracket, line_num) in &self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack {\n933\t            let error_type = match *bracket {\n934\t                '[' => DelimiterErrorType::UnclosedBracket,\n935\t                '{' => \"delimiter_balance\".to_string(),\n940\t                format!(\"{}: {}\", error_type.code(), error_type.description())\n941\t            ).with_line(*line_num).with_delimiter_error_type(error_type));\n942\t        }\n943\t\n944\t        // Check for unclosed quotes\n945\t        if self.delimiter_state.quote_state.is_some() {\n946\t Some(QuoteType::Double) => (DelimiterErrorType::UnclosedDoubleQuote, \"\\\"\"),\n949\t                None => unreachable!(),\n950\t            };\n951\t\n952\t            errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n953\t                \"quote_balance\".to_string(),\n954\t                format!(\"{}: {}\", error_type.code(), error_type.description())\n955\t            ).with_line(0).with_delimiter_error_type(error_type)); // Line 0 since we don't track where it opened\n956\t        }\n957\t    }\n958\t\n959\t    /// Finalize structure checks after processing all  }\n963\t\n964\t    /// Get leading whitespace length from a line\n965\t    fn get_leading_whitespace_length(&self, line: &str) -> usize {\n966\t        line.chars().take_while(|c| c.is_whitespace()).count()\n967\t    }\n968\t\n969\t    /// Check if a line looks like a key definition\n970\t    fn looks_like_key(&self, trimmed: &str) -> bool {\n971\t        // Keys are typically alphanumeric with underscores, dashes, or dots\n972\t        // They don't start with special YAML characters\n973\t        if trimmed.is_empty() {\n974\t            return false;\n975\t        }\n976\t\n977\t        let first_char = trimmed.chars().next().unwrap();\n978\t\n979\t        // Exclude lines that start with YAML special characters\n980\t        if matches!(first_char, '-' | ':' | '?' | '|' | '>' | '#' | '[' | ']' | '{' | '}' | '\"' | '\\'' | '!' | '&' | '*' | '%' | '@' | '`') {\n981\t            return false;\n982\t        }\n983\t\n984\t        // Look for word characters at the start\n985\t        trimmed.chars().next().map_or(false, |c| c.is_alphanumeric() || c == '_' || c == '.')\n986\t    }\n987\t}\n988\t\n989\timpl Default for SyntaxDetector {\n990\t    fn default()
