YAML Syntax Error Detection Module\n//!\n//! This module provides comprehensive syntax error detection for YAML files.\n//! It detects three main categories of errors:\n//!\n//! 1. **Indentation Errors**: Mixed spaces/tabs, inconsistent indentation levels\n//! 2. **Delimiter Errors**: Missing colons, unbalanced brackets/braces, quote errors\n//! 3. **Structure Errors**: Invalid mappings, malformed sequences, duplicate keys\n//!\n//! ## Architecture\n//!\n//! The detector follows a line-by-line analysis approach:\n//!\n//! - LineTracker: Tracks line numbers and content for error reporting\n//! - IndentationDetector: Analyzes indentation patterns\n//! - DelimiterDetector: Checks delimiter consistency\n//! - StructureDetector: Validates YAML structure rules\n//!\n//! ## Usage\n//!\n//! ```ignore\n//! use armor::parsers::yaml::syntax_detector::{SyntaxDetector, SyntaxError};\n//!\n//! let detector = SyntaxDetector::new();\n//! let yaml_content = \"key: value\\n  nested: value\";\n//! let errors = detector.detect_errors(yaml_content);\n//!\n//! for error in errors {\n//!     println!(\"Line {}: {}\", error.line, error.message);\n//! }\n//! ```\n\nuse crate::parsers::yaml::types::{ValidationError, ValidationResult};\nuse crate::parsers::yaml::scope::{ScopeStack as ScopeScopeStack, DuplicateKeyError};\nuse std::collections::{HashMap, HashSet};\nuse std::fmt;\n\n/// Classification of indentation error types\n///\n/// This enum provides structured categorization of different indentation\n/// error types that can occur in YAML files.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub enum IndentationErrorType {\n    /// Mixed tabs and spaces in the same line\n    MixedTabsAndSpaces,\n    /// Indentation not a multiple of the base indent size\n    InvalidIndentLevel,\n    /// Indentation increase is too large (> 2 levels at once)\n    ExcessiveIndentIncrease,\n    /// Indentation increase is not a multiple of base indent size\n    InvalidIndentIncrease,\n    /// Tab character detected (when tabs are not allowed)\n    TabCharacter,\n}\n\nimpl IndentationErrorType {\n    /// Get a human-readable description of this error type\n    pub fn description(&self) -> &'static str {\n        match self {\n            Self::MixedTabsAndSpaces => \"mixed tabs and spaces in indentation\",\n            Self::InvalidIndentLevel => \"indentation level is not a multiple of base indent size\",\n            Self::ExcessiveIndentIncrease => \"indentation increase is too large\",\n            Self::InvalidIndentIncrease => \"indentation increase is not a multiple of base indent size\",\n            Self::TabCharacter => \"tab character detected in indentation\",\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get a short code for this error type\n    pub fn code(&self) -> &'static str {\n        match self {\n            Self::MixedTabsAndSpaces => \"E001\",\n            Self::InvalidIndentLevel => \"E002\",\n            Self::ExcessiveIndentIncrease => \"E003\",\n            Self::InvalidIndentIncrease => \"E004\",\n            Self::TabCharacter => \"E005\",\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get all indentation error types\n    pub fn all() -> &'static [Self] {\n        &[\n            Self::MixedTabsAndSpaces,\n            Self::InvalidIndentLevel,\n            Self::ExcessiveIndentIncrease,\n            Self::InvalidIndentIncrease,\n            Self::TabCharacter,\n        ]\n    }\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Display for IndentationErrorType {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        write!(f, \"{}: {}\", self.code(), self.description())\n    }\n}\n\n/// Classification of delimiter error types\n///\n/// This enum provides structured categorization of different delimiter\n/// error types that can occur in YAML files.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub enum DelimiterErrorType {\n    /// Missing colon after a key\n    MissingColon,\n    /// Unmatched opening bracket '['\n    UnmatchedOpeningBracket,\n    /// Unmatched closing bracket ']'\n    UnmatchedClosingBracket,\n    /// Unclosed bracket '['\n    UnclosedBracket,\n    /// Unmatched opening brace '{'\n    UnmatchedOpeningBrace,\n    /// Unmatched closing brace '}'\n    UnmatchedClosingBrace,\n    /// Unclosed brace '{'\n    UnclosedBrace,\n    /// Mismatched quotes (single inside double or vice versa)\n    MismatchedQuotes,\n    /// Unclosed single quote\n    UnclosedSingleQuote,\n    /// Unclosed double quote\n    UnclosedDoubleQuote,\n}\n\nimpl DelimiterErrorType {\n    /// Get a human-readable description of this error type\n    pub fn description(&self) -> &'static str {\n        match self {\n            Self::MissingColon => \"missing colon after key\",\n            Self::UnmatchedOpeningBracket => \"unmatched opening bracket '['\",\n            Self::UnmatchedClosingBracket => \"unmatched closing bracket ']'\",\n            Self::UnclosedBracket => \"unclosed bracket '['\",\n            Self::UnmatchedOpeningBrace => \"unmatched opening brace '{'\",\n            Self::UnmatchedClosingBrace => \"unmatched closing brace '}'\",\n            Self::UnclosedBrace => \"unclosed brace '{'\",\n            Self::MismatchedQuotes => \"mismatched quotes\",\n            Self::UnclosedSingleQuote => \"unclosed single quote\",\n            Self::UnclosedDoubleQuote => \"unclosed double quote\",\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get a short code for this error type\n    pub fn code(&self) -> &'static str {\n        match self {\n            Self::MissingColon => \"D001\",\n            Self::UnmatchedOpeningBracket => \"D002\",\n            Self::UnmatchedClosingBracket => \"D003\",\n            Self::UnclosedBracket => \"D004\",\n            Self::UnmatchedOpeningBrace => \"D005\",\n            Self::UnmatchedClosingBrace => \"D006\",\n            Self::UnclosedBrace => \"D007\",\n            Self::MismatchedQuotes => \"D008\",\n            Self::UnclosedSingleQuote => \"D009\",\n            Self::UnclosedDoubleQuote => \"D010\",\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get all delimiter error types\n    pub fn all() -> &'static [Self] {\n        &[\n            Self::MissingColon,\n            Self::UnmatchedOpeningBracket,\n            Self::UnmatchedClosingBracket,\n            Self::UnclosedBracket,\n            Self::UnmatchedOpeningBrace,\n            Self::UnmatchedClosingBrace,\n            Self::UnclosedBrace,\n            Self::MismatchedQuotes,\n            Self::UnclosedSingleQuote,\n            Self::UnclosedDoubleQuote,\n        ]\n    }\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Display for DelimiterErrorType {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        write!(f, \"{}: {}\", self.code(), self.description())\n    }\n}\n\n/// Main syntax detector for YAML content\n///\n/// This struct orchestrates all syntax detection checks and provides\n/// a unified interface for error detection.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct SyntaxDetector {\n    /// Configuration for detection behavior\n    config: DetectorConfig,\n    /// Track indentation state across lines\n    indentation_state: IndentationState,\n    /// Track delimiter state (brackets, braces, quotes)\n    delimiter_state: DelimiterState,\n    /// Track structure state (keys, sequences, mappings)\n    structure_state: StructureState,\n}\n\n/// Configuration for syntax detection behavior\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct DetectorConfig {\n    /// Whether to detect tab/space mixing\n    pub detect_mixed_indentation: bool,\n    /// Whether to check for consistent indentation (multiples of base)\n    pub check_consistent_indentation: bool,\n    /// Base indentation size (usually 2 or 4)\n    pub base_indent_size: usize,\n    /// Whether to validate delimiter balancing\n    pub validate_delimiter_balance: bool,\n    /// Whether to detect duplicate keys\n    pub detect_duplicate_keys: bool,\n    /// Whether to detect invalid sequence syntax\n    pub detect_invalid_sequences: bool,\n    /// Whether to detect invalid mapping syntax\n    pub detect_invalid_mappings: bool,\n}\n\nimpl Default for DetectorConfig {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            detect_mixed_indentation: true,\n            check_consistent_indentation: true,\n            base_indent_size: 2,\n            validate_delimiter_balance: true,\n            detect_duplicate_keys: true,\n            detect_invalid_sequences: true,\n            detect_invalid_mappings: true,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// State tracking for indentation analysis\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\nstruct IndentationState {\n    /// Whether tabs have been seen in the file\n    has_tabs: bool,\n    /// Whether spaces have been seen in the file\n    has_spaces: bool,\n    /// Previous line's indentation level (in spaces)\n    prev_indent_level: usize,\n    /// Expected indentation levels for nested contexts\n    indent_stack: Vec<usize>,\n    /// Lines with mixed indentation\n    mixed_lines: Vec<usize>,\n}\n\n/// State tracking for delimiter analysis\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\nstruct DelimiterState {\n    /// Stack of opening brackets/braces\n    bracket_stack: Vec<(char, usize)>, // (bracket_type, line_number)\n    /// Current quote state (None, Single, Double)\n    quote_state: Option<QuoteType>,\n    /// Lines with quote errors\n    quote_errors: Vec<usize>,\n    /// Whether we're inside a multiline block (|, >)\n    in_multiline_block: bool,\n    /// Indentation level of the multiline block start\n    multiline_block_indent: usize,\n    /// Whether we're inside flow-style context (within [] or {})\n    in_flow_context: bool,\n}\n\n/// Quote type for tracking\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\nenum QuoteType {\n    Single,\n    Double,\n}\n\n/// A scope representing a mapping context at a specific nesting level\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\nstruct Scope {\n    /// Indentation level (number of leading spaces)\n    indent_level: usize,\n    /// Keys defined within this scope\n    keys: HashSet<String>,\n    /// Line number where this scope started (for error reporting)\n    start_line: usize,\n    /// Parent key that created this scope (e.g., \"web\" in \"services: {...}\")\n    parent_key: Option<String>,\n    /// Whether this scope is in flow-style mapping ({key: value})\n    is_flow_style: bool,\n    /// Whether this scope is within a sequence context\n    in_sequence_context: bool,\n    /// Unique identifier for sequence items to distinguish them at same indent\n    sequence_item_id: Option<usize>,\n}\n\nimpl Scope {\n    /// Create a new scope\n    fn new(indent_level: usize, start_line: usize, parent_key: Option<String>) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            indent_level,\n            keys: HashSet::new(),\n            start_line,\n            parent_key,\n            is_flow_style: false,\n            in_sequence_context: false,\n            sequence_item_id: None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Add a key to this scope, returning true if it's a duplicate\n    fn add_key(&mut self, key: &str) -> bool {\n        !self.keys.insert(key.to_string())\n    }\n\n    /// Check if this scope contains a key\n    fn contains_key(&self, key: &str) -> bool {\n        self.keys.contains(key)\n    }\n\n    /// Check if this scope is equivalent to another for duplicate key purposes\n    /// (same indent level and same sequence item)\n    fn is_same_scope_as(&self, other: &Scope) -> bool {\n        self.indent_level == other.indent_level\n            && self.in_sequence_context == other.in_sequence_context\n            && self.sequence_item_id == other.sequence_item_id\n    }\n}\n\n/// Hierarchical stack of active scopes\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\nstruct ScopeStack {\n    /// Stack of active scopes (top = current scope)\n    scopes: Vec<Scope>,\n    /// Base indentation size (usually 2 or 4 spaces)\n    base_indent: usize,\n    /// Sequence item counter for generating unique IDs\n    sequence_item_counter: usize,\n}\n\nimpl ScopeStack {\n    /// Create a new scope stack\n    fn new(base_indent: usize) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            scopes: vec![Scope::new(0, 0, None)], // Root scope\n            base_indent,\n            sequence_item_counter: 0,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get the current scope (top of stack)\n    fn current_scope(&mut self) -> &mut Scope {\n        self.scopes.last_mut().expect(\"Scope stack should never be empty\")\n    }\n\n    /// Get scope for a specific indentation level\n    fn get_scope_at_level(&self, indent_level: usize) -> Option<&Scope> {\n        self.scopes.iter().find(|s| s.indent_level == indent_level)\n    }\n\n    /// Enter a new scope (when indent increases)\n    fn enter_scope(&mut self, indent_level: usize, line: usize, parent_key: Option<String>) {\n        // Remove all scopes deeper than this level (fresh start for sibling mappings)\n        self.scopes.retain(|s| s.indent_level < indent_level);\n\n        // Create a fresh scope at this level\n        let new_scope = Scope::new(indent_level, line, parent_key);\n        self.scopes.push(new_scope);\n    }\n\n    /// Exit to parent scope (when indent decreases)\n    fn exit_to_scope(&mut self, target_indent: usize) {\n        // Remove all scopes deeper than target\n        self.scopes.retain(|s| s.indent_level <= target_indent);\n    }\n\n    /// Check if current scope contains a key\n    fn contains_key(&self, key: &str) -> bool {\n        self.scopes.last()\n            .map(|scope| scope.contains_key(key))\n            .unwrap_or(false)\n    }\n\n    /// Add a key to current scope\n    fn add_key(&mut self, key: &str) {\n        let scope = self.current_scope();\n        scope.add_key(key);\n    }\n\n    /// Get human-readable path to current scope\n    fn get_scope_path(&self) -> String {\n        let mut path = Vec::new();\n        for scope in &self.scopes {\n            if let Some(ref key) = scope.parent_key {\n                path.push(key.clone());\n            }\n        }\n        path.join(\".\")\n    }\n\n    /// Get current indent level\n    fn current_indent(&self) -> usize {\n        self.scopes.last()\n            .map(|scope| scope.indent_level)\n            .unwrap_or(0)\n    }\n\n    /// Enter a sequence context (when we see a `-` item)\n    fn enter_sequence_scope(&mut self, indent_level: usize, line: usize) {\n        // Remove all scopes deeper than this level\n        self.scopes.retain(|s| s.indent_level < indent_level);\n\n        // Check if there's already a scope at this level that's in a sequence context\n        let needs_new_scope = self.scopes.last()\n            .map(|scope| scope.indent_level != indent_level || !scope.in_sequence_context)\n            .unwrap_or(true);\n\n        if needs_new_scope {\n            // Create a new scope for this sequence item\n            let mut new_scope = Scope::new(indent_level, line, None);\n            new_scope.in_sequence_context = true;\n            self.sequence_item_counter += 1;\n            new_scope.sequence_item_id = Some(self.sequence_item_counter);\n            self.scopes.push(new_scope);\n        } else {\n            // Reset the existing scope for a new sequence item\n            if let Some(scope) = self.scopes.last_mut() {\n                scope.keys.clear();\n                scope.start_line = line;\n                self.sequence_item_counter += 1;\n                scope.sequence_item_id = Some(self.sequence_item_counter);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check if we're in a sequence context\n    fn in_sequence_context(&self) -> bool {\n        self.scopes.last()\n            .map(|scope| scope.in_sequence_context)\n            .unwrap_or(false)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Default for ScopeStack {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::new(2) // Default base indent of 2 spaces\n    }\n}\n\n/// State tracking for structure analysis\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\nstruct StructureState {\n    /// Stack of nested structures (mapping, sequence, etc.)\n    context_stack: Vec<StructureContext>,\n    /// Hierarchical scope-based key tracking\n    scope_stack: ScopeStack,\n    /// Previous line's indentation level\n    prev_indent: usize,\n}\n\n/// Current structure context\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\nenum StructureContext {\n    Mapping,\n    Sequence,\n    FlowMapping,\n    FlowSequence,\n}\n\nimpl SyntaxDetector {\n    /// Create a new syntax detector with default configuration\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        Self::with_config(DetectorConfig::default())\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new syntax detector with custom configuration\n    pub fn with_config(config: DetectorConfig) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            config,\n            indentation_state: IndentationState::default(),\n            delimiter_state: DelimiterState::default(),\n            structure_state: StructureState::default(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Detect all syntax errors in YAML content\n    ///\n    /// This is the main entry point for syntax detection. It runs all\n    /// enabled detectors and returns a comprehensive list of errors.\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    /// * `content` - The YAML content to analyze\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    /// A vector of validation errors representing all detected syntax issues\n    pub fn detect_errors(&mut self, content: &str) -> Vec<ValidationError> {\n        let mut errors = Vec::new();\n        let lines: Vec<&str> = content.lines().collect();\n\n        // Reset state for new detection\n        self.reset_state();\n\n        for (line_num, line) in lines.iter().enumerate() {\n            let line_num_1index = line_num + 1; // Convert to 1-indexed\n\n            // Skip empty lines and comments for most checks\n            let is_meaningful = !line.trim().is_empty() && !line.trim().starts_with('#');\n\n            if is_meaningful {\n                // Run all enabled detectors\n                if self.config.detect_mixed_indentation || self.config.check_consistent_indentation {\n                    self.detect_indentation_errors(line, line_num_1index, &mut errors);\n                }\n\n                if self.config.validate_delimiter_balance {\n                    self.detect_delimiter_errors(line, line_num_1index, &mut errors);\n                }\n\n                if self.config.detect_invalid_sequences || self.config.detect_invalid_mappings {\n                    self.detect_structure_errors(line, line_num_1index, &mut errors);\n                }\n\n                if self.config.detect_duplicate_keys {\n                    self.detect_duplicate_key_errors(line, line_num_1index, &mut errors);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Final validation checks\n        self.finalize_delimiter_checks(&mut errors);\n        self.finalize_structure_checks(&mut errors);\n\n        errors\n    }\n\n    /// Detect syntax errors and convert to ValidationResult\n    pub fn detect_to_validation_result(&mut self, content: &str) -> ValidationResult {\n        let errors = self.detect_errors(content);\n\n        if errors.is_empty() {\n            ValidationResult::success()\n        } else {\n            ValidationResult::failure(errors)\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Reset all state tracking for a new detection run\n    fn reset_state(&mut self) {\n        self.indentation_state = IndentationState::default();\n        self.delimiter_state = DelimiterState::default();\n        self.structure_state = StructureState::default();\n    }\n\n    /// Detect indentation-related errors\n    fn detect_indentation_errors(&mut self, line: &str, line_num: usize, errors: &mut Vec<ValidationError>) {\n        let leading_whitespace_len = self.get_leading_whitespace_length(line);\n\n        // Check for mixed tabs and spaces in leading whitespace\n        if self.config.detect_mixed_indentation && leading_whitespace_len > 0 {\n            let leading_chars: Vec<char> = line.chars().take(leading_whitespace_len).collect();\n            let has_tabs = leading_chars.contains(&'\\t');\n            let has_spaces = leading_chars.contains(&' ');\n\n            if has_tabs && has_spaces {\n                self.indentation_state.has_tabs = true;\n                self.indentation_state.has_spaces = true;\n                self.indentation_state.mixed_lines.push(line_num);\n\n                let error_type = IndentationErrorType::MixedTabsAndSpaces;\n                errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n                    error_type.description()\n                ).with_line(line_num).with_indentation_error_type(error_type));\n            } else if has_tabs {\n                self.indentation_state.has_tabs = true;\n                // Optionally report tab-only indentation\n                if self.config.detect_mixed_indentation {\n                    let error_type = IndentationErrorType::TabCharacter;\n                    errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                        format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n                        format!(\"{}: {}\", error_type.code(), error_type.description())\n                    ).with_line(line_num).with_indentation_error_type(error_type));\n                }\n            } else if has_spaces {\n                self.indentation_state.has_spaces = true;\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Check for consistent indentation levels\n        if self.config.check_consistent_indentation && leading_whitespace_len > 0 {\n            let indent_level = leading_whitespace_len;\n\n            // Check if indentation is a multiple of base_indent_size\n            if indent_level % self.config.base_indent_size != 0 {\n                let error_type = IndentationErrorType::InvalidIndentLevel;\n                errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n                    format!(\"{}: {} spaces is not a multiple of {}\",\n                            error_type.code(), indent_level, self.config.base_indent_size)\n                ).with_line(line_num).with_indentation_error_type(error_type));\n            }\n\n            // Check if indentation increase is consistent\n            if indent_level > self.indentation_state.prev_indent_level {\n                let increase = indent_level - self.indentation_state.prev_indent_level;\n\n                // Check if increase is too large (more than 2 levels at once)\n                if increase > self.config.base_indent_size * 2 {\n                    let error_type = IndentationErrorType::ExcessiveIndentIncrease;\n                    errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                        format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n                        format!(\"{}: increase of {} spaces exceeds maximum of {} levels\",\n                                error_type.code(), increase, self.config.base_indent_size * 2)\n                    ).with_line(line_num).with_indentation_error_type(error_type));\n                }\n\n                if increase % self.config.base_indent_size != 0 {\n                    let error_type = IndentationErrorType::InvalidIndentIncrease;\n                    errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                        format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n                        format!(\"{}: increase of {} spaces is not a multiple of {}\",\n                                error_type.code(), increase, self.config.base_indent_size)\n                    ).with_line(line_num).with_indentation_error_type(error_type));\n                } else {\n                    // Valid increase - push to stack\n                    self.indentation_state.indent_stack.push(indent_level);\n                }\n            } else if indent_level < self.indentation_state.prev_indent_level {\n                // Decrease in indentation - pop from stack\n                while !self.indentation_state.indent_stack.is_empty()\n                      && *self.indentation_state.indent_stack.last().unwrap() > indent_level {\n                    self.indentation_state.indent_stack.pop();\n                }\n            }\n\n            self.indentation_state.prev_indent_level = indent_level;\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Detect delimiter-related errors\n    fn detect_delimiter_errors(&mut self, line: &str, line_num: usize, errors: &mut Vec<ValidationError>) {\n        let leading_whitespace_len = self.get_leading_whitespace_length(line);\n        let trimmed = line.trim();\n\n        // Check if we're inside a multiline block\n        if self.delimiter_state.in_multiline_block {\n            // Multiline blocks continue until we find a line with less or equal indentation\n            // that's not empty or a comment\n            if leading_whitespace_len > self.delimiter_state.multiline_block_indent ||\n               (leading_whitespace_len == self.delimiter_state.multiline_block_indent && (trimmed.is_empty() || trimmed.starts_with('#'))) {\n                return; // Skip lines inside the multiline block\n            } else {\n                // Exit multiline block when indentation decreases\n                self.delimiter_state.in_multiline_block = false;\n                self.delimiter_state.multiline_block_indent = 0;\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Skip empty lines and comments (but not when inside multiline blocks - handled above)\n        if trimmed.is_empty() || trimmed.starts_with('#') {\n            return;\n        }\n\n        // Handle document markers (---, ...)\n        if trimmed == \"---\" || trimmed == \"...\" {\n            return; // Document markers should not be flagged for missing colons\n        }\n\n        // Handle multiline block scalars (|, >, |-, |- , >-, >+, |+)\n        // These appear after a colon, like \"key: |\" or \"key: >\"\n        if trimmed.contains(\": |\") || trimmed.contains(\": >\") ||\n           trimmed.contains(\":|-\") || trimmed.contains(\":>-\") ||\n           trimmed.contains(\":|+\") || trimmed.contains(\":>+\") {\n            self.delimiter_state.in_multiline_block = true;\n            self.delimiter_state.multiline_block_indent = leading_whitespace_len; // Content must be MORE indented than this line\n        }\n\n        // Check for missing colons after keys\n        if !trimmed.starts_with('-') && // Not a sequence item\n           !trimmed.starts_with('?') && // Not an explicit key\n           !trimmed.starts_with(':') && // Not a value\n           !trimmed.starts_with('&') && // Not an anchor\n           !trimmed.starts_with('*') && // Not an alias\n           !trimmed.starts_with('!') && // Not a tag\n           !trimmed.starts_with('|') && // Not a literal block scalar\n           !trimmed.starts_with('>') && // Not a folded block scalar\n           !trimmed.contains('{') && // Not flow style mapping\n           !trimmed.contains('[') && // Not flow style sequence\n           !trimmed.contains(':') && // No colon at all\n           !trimmed.is_empty() &&\n           self.looks_like_key(trimmed) {\n\n            let error_type = DelimiterErrorType::MissingColon;\n            errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n                format!(\"{}: {}\", error_type.code(), error_type.description())\n            ).with_line(line_num).with_delimiter_error_type(error_type));\n        }\n\n        // Track bracket/brace balance and flow context\n        for (char_pos, ch) in line.chars().enumerate() {\n            match ch {\n                '[' => {\n                    self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack.push(('[', line_num));\n                    self.delimiter_state.in_flow_context = true;\n                }\n                ']' => {\n                    if let Some(('[', _)) = self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack.last() {\n                        self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack.pop();\n                        // Update flow context when stack becomes empty\n                        self.delimiter_state.in_flow_context = !self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack.is_empty();\n                    } else {\n                        let error_type = DelimiterErrorType::UnmatchedClosingBracket;\n                        errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                            format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n                            format!(\"{}: {}\", error_type.code(), error_type.description())\n                        ).with_line(line_num).with_delimiter_error_type(error_type));\n                    }\n                }\n                '{' => {\n                    self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack.push(('{', line_num));\n                    self.delimiter_state.in_flow_context = true;\n                }\n                '}' => {\n                    if let Some(('{', _)) = self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack.last() {\n                        self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack.pop();\n                        // Update flow context when stack becomes empty\n                        self.delimiter_state.in_flow_context = !self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack.is_empty();\n                    } else {\n                        let error_type = DelimiterErrorType::UnmatchedClosingBrace;\n                        errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                            format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n                            format!(\"{}: {}\", error_type.code(), error_type.description())\n                        ).with_line(line_num).with_delimiter_error_type(error_type));\n                    }\n                }\n                '\\'' => {\n                    match self.delimiter_state.quote_state {\n                        None => self.delimiter_state.quote_state = Some(QuoteType::Single),\n                        Some(QuoteType::Single) => self.delimiter_state.quote_state = None,\n                        Some(QuoteType::Double) => {\n                            // Mismatched quote\n                            let error_type = DelimiterErrorType::MismatchedQuotes;\n                            errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n                                format!(\"{}: {} (single inside double)\", error_type.code(), error_type.description())\n                            ).with_line(line_num).with_delimiter_error_type(error_type));\n                            self.delimiter_state.quote_errors.push(line_num);\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n                '\"' => {\n                    match self.delimiter_state.quote_state {\n                        None => self.delimiter_state.quote_state = Some(QuoteType::Double),\n                        Some(QuoteType::Double) => self.delimiter_state.quote_state = None,\n                        Some(QuoteType::Single) => {\n                            // Mismatched quote\n                            let error_type = DelimiterErrorType::MismatchedQuotes;\n                            errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n                                format!(\"{}: {} (double inside single)\", error_type.code(), error_type.description())\n                            ).with_line(line_num).with_delimiter_error_type(error_type));\n                            self.delimiter_state.quote_errors.push(line_num);\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n                _ => {}\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Detect structure-related errors\n    fn detect_structure_errors(&mut self, line: &str, line_num: usize, errors: &mut Vec<ValidationError>) {\n        let trimmed = line.trim();\n\n        // Detect invalid sequence syntax\n        if self.config.detect_invalid_sequences {\n            if trimmed.starts_with(\"- \") {\n                // Valid sequence item\n                if self.structure_state.context_stack.is_empty() ||\n                   !matches!(self.structure_state.context_stack.last(), Some(StructureContext::Sequence)) {\n                    self.structure_state.context_stack.push(StructureContext::Sequence);\n                }\n            } else if trimmed.starts_with('-') && trimmed.len() > 1 {\n                // Dash followed by something other than space\n                if let Some(next_char) = trimmed.chars().nth(1) {\n                    if !next_char.is_whitespace() {\n                        errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                            format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n                            \"sequence dash '-' must be followed by whitespace\"\n                        ).with_line(line_num));\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Detect invalid mapping syntax\n        if self.config.detect_invalid_mappings {\n            if let Some(colon_pos) = trimmed.find(':') {\n                let before_colon = &trimmed[..colon_pos];\n\n                // Check if colon is properly used after a key\n                if !before_colon.is_empty() && !before_colon.ends_with(' ') {\n                    // Valid key: value pair\n                    if self.structure_state.context_stack.is_empty() ||\n                       !matches!(self.structure_state.context_stack.last(), Some(StructureContext::Mapping)) {\n                        self.structure_state.context_stack.push(StructureContext::Mapping);\n                    }\n                } else if before_colon.is_empty() {\n                    // Colon at start - might be a value or error\n                    if !trimmed.starts_with(\": \") && trimmed != \":\" {\n                        errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                            format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n                            \"colon ':' at start must be followed by space\"\n                        ).with_line(line_num));\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Check for flow syntax indicators\n        if trimmed.contains('{') || trimmed.contains('[') {\n            self.structure_state.context_stack.push(if trimmed.contains('{') {\n                StructureContext::FlowMapping\n            } else {\n                StructureContext::FlowSequence\n            });\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Detect duplicate key errors using scope-aware tracking\n    fn detect_duplicate_key_errors(&mut self, line: &str, line_num: usize, errors: &mut Vec<ValidationError>) {\n        // Skip duplicate key detection when inside flow-style contexts ([] or {})\n        // Flow-style YAML uses {key: value} syntax which should not be treated as duplicate keys\n        if self.delimiter_state.in_flow_context {\n            return;\n        }\n\n        let trimmed = line.trim();\n        let indent = self.get_leading_whitespace_length(line);\n\n        // Check if this is a sequence item with a key-value pair (e.g., \"- key: value\")\n        // This creates a new scope for each sequence item\n        if trimmed.starts_with(\"- \") {\n            if let Some(colon_pos) = trimmed.find(':') {\n                // We have \"- key: value\" format\n                let key_part = &trimmed[2..colon_pos]; // Skip \"- \" to get the key\n                let key = key_part.trim();\n\n                // Only enter sequence scope if it looks like a valid mapping key\n                if !key.is_empty() && !key.starts_with('\\'') && !key.starts_with('\"')\n                    && !key.contains('#') && !key.contains('?') {\n                    self.structure_state.scope_stack.enter_sequence_scope(indent, line_num);\n                }\n            }\n            // Sequence items don't need duplicate checking at this level\n            // The keys within the sequence item will be checked when we process them\n            return;\n        }\n\n        // Extract key if this is a key-value pair\n        if let Some(colon_pos) = trimmed.find(':') {\n            let key_part = &trimmed[..colon_pos];\n\n            // Check if this is a parent key (ends with colon and no value on same line)\n            // A parent key typically has nothing after the colon or just whitespace/comments\n            let after_colon = trimmed[colon_pos + 1..].trim();\n            let is_parent_key = after_colon.is_empty() ||\n                                after_colon.starts_with('#');\n\n            if is_parent_key {\n                // This is a parent key - extract the key name and enter a new scope\n                if !key_part.is_empty() && !key_part.contains('-') && !key_part.contains('?') {\n                    let key = key_part.trim();\n                    if !key.starts_with('\\'') && !key.starts_with('\"') && !key.contains('#') {\n                        // Enter a new scope for the parent key's nested content\n                        self.structure_state.scope_stack.enter_scope(\n                            indent + self.config.base_indent_size,\n                            line_num,\n                            Some(key.to_string())\n                        );\n                    }\n                }\n                return;\n            }\n\n            // Skip if it's a value or not a simple key\n            if key_part.is_empty() || key_part.contains('-') || key_part.contains('?') {\n                return;\n            }\n\n            let key = key_part.trim();\n\n            // Skip quoted keys and special keys\n            if key.starts_with('\\'') || key.starts_with('\"') || key.contains('#') {\n                return;\n            }\n\n            // Handle scope transitions based on indentation changes\n            use std::cmp::Ordering;\n            match indent.cmp(&self.structure_state.scope_stack.current_indent()) {\n                Ordering::Greater => {\n                    // Indent increased - this should be a parent key (handled above)\n                    // If we get here, it's a regular key at deeper indent without a parent\n                    // Enter an anonymous scope\n                    self.structure_state.scope_stack.enter_scope(indent, line_num, None);\n                }\n                Ordering::Less => {\n                    // Indent decreased - exit to the appropriate parent scope\n                    self.structure_state.scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n                }\n                Ordering::Equal => {\n                    // Same scope level - continue checking for duplicates\n                }\n            }\n\n            // Check for duplicates in the current scope\n            if self.structure_state.scope_stack.contains_key(key) {\n                let scope_path = self.structure_state.scope_stack.get_scope_path();\n                let error_message = if scope_path.is_empty() {\n                    format!(\"duplicate key '{}' in mapping scope\", key)\n                } else {\n                    format!(\"duplicate key '{}' in mapping scope '{}'\", key, scope_path)\n                };\n                errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                    format!(\"key_{}\", key),\n                    error_message\n                ).with_line(line_num));\n            } else {\n                self.structure_state.scope_stack.add_key(key);\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Update previous indentation for next comparison\n        self.structure_state.prev_indent = indent;\n    }\n\n    /// Finalize delimiter checks after processing all lines\n    fn finalize_delimiter_checks(&mut self, errors: &mut Vec<ValidationError>) {\n        // Check for unclosed brackets\n        for (bracket, line_num) in &self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack {\n            let error_type = match *bracket {\n                '[' => DelimiterErrorType::UnclosedBracket,\n                '{' => DelimiterErrorType::UnclosedBrace,\n                _ => continue, // Skip unknown bracket types\n            };\n            errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                \"delimiter_balance\".to_string(),\n                format!(\"{}: {}\", error_type.code(), error_type.description())\n            ).with_line(*line_num).with_delimiter_error_type(error_type));\n        }\n\n        // Check for unclosed quotes\n        if self.delimiter_state.quote_state.is_some() {\n            let (error_type, quote_char) = match self.delimiter_state.quote_state {\n                Some(QuoteType::Single) => (DelimiterErrorType::UnclosedSingleQuote, \"'\"),\n                Some(QuoteType::Double) => (DelimiterErrorType::UnclosedDoubleQuote, \"\\\"\"),\n                None => unreachable!(),\n            };\n\n            errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                \"quote_balance\".to_string(),\n                format!(\"{}: {}\", error_type.code(), error_type.description())\n            ).with_line(0).with_delimiter_error_type(error_type)); // Line 0 since we don't track where it opened\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Finalize structure checks after processing all lines\n    fn finalize_structure_checks(&mut self, _errors: &mut Vec<ValidationError>) {\n        // No-op - same-level duplicate detection is handled in detect_duplicate_key_errors\n    }\n\n    /// Get leading whitespace length from a line\n    fn get_leading_whitespace_length(&self, line: &str) -> usize {\n        line.chars().take_while(|c| c.is_whitespace()).count()\n    }\n\n    /// Check if a line looks like a key definition\n    fn looks_like_key(&self, trimmed: &str) -> bool {\n        // Keys are typically alphanumeric with underscores, dashes, or dots\n        // They don't start with special YAML characters\n        if trimmed.is_empty() {\n            return false;\n        }\n\n        let first_char = trimmed.chars().next().unwrap();\n\n        // Exclude lines that start with YAML special characters\n        if matches!(first_char, '-' | ':' | '?' | '|' | '>' | '#' | '[' | ']' | '{' | '}' | '\"' | '\\'' | '!' | '&' | '*' | '%' | '@' | '`') {\n            return false;\n        }\n\n        // Look for word characters at the start\n        trimmed.chars().next().map_or(false, |c| c.is_alphanumeric() || c == '_' || c == '.')\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Default for SyntaxDetector {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::new()\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_detect_mixed_indentation() {\n        let mut detector = SyntaxDetector::new();\n        let yaml = \"key: value\\n\\t  bad: value\";\n        let errors = detector.detect_errors(yaml);\n\n        assert!(!errors.is_empty());\n        assert!(errors.iter().any(|e| e.message.contains(\"mixed tabs and spaces\")));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_detect_missing_colon() {\n        let mut detector = SyntaxDetector::new();\n        let yaml = \"key value\\n  key2: value2\";\n        let errors = detector.detect_errors(yaml);\n\n        assert!(!errors.is_empty());\n        assert!(errors.iter().any(|e| e.message.contains(\"missing colon\")));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_detect_unmatched_bracket() {\n        let mut detector = SyntaxDetector::new();\n        let yaml = \"key: [value1, value2\";\n        let errors = detector.detect_errors(yaml);\n\n        assert!(!errors.is_empty());\n        assert!(errors.iter().any(|e| e.message.contains(\"unclosed\")));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_valid_yaml_no_errors() {\n        let mut detector = SyntaxDetector::new();\n        let yaml = \"key: value\\n  nested:\\n    - item1\\n    - item2\";\n        let errors = detector.detect_errors(yaml);\n\n        assert!(errors.is_empty());\n    }\n}\n","numLines":1038,"startLine":1,"totalLines":1038}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_c693db4cb48a44aead5d80b2","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Scope representation data structures\n2\t//!\n3\t//! This module defines the core data structures for representing hierarchical scopes\n4\t//! in YAML documents. These structures enable accurate duplicate key detection by\n5\t//! tracking keys within their proper scope contexts.\n6\t//!\n7\t//! # Overview\n8\t//!\n9\t//! YAML documents use indentation to define nested scopes. Keys in different scopes\n10\t//! can have the same name without being duplicates. For example:\n11\t//!\n12\t//! ```yaml\n13\t//! services:\n14\t//!   web:\n15\t//!     host: localhost    # \"host\" in services.web scope\n16\t//!     port: 8080\n17\t//!   database:\n18\t//!     host: db.example.com  # \"host\" in services.database scope (NOT a duplicate)\n19\t//!     port: 5432\n20\t//! ```\n21\t//!\n22\t//! The scope representation system tracks:\n23\t//! - **Scope**: A mapping context at a specific nesting level\n24\t//! - **ScopeStack**: A hierarchical stack of active scopes during parsing\n25\t//! - **KeyContext**: Classification of key types (inline scalar, parent mapping, parent sequence)\n26\t//!\n27\t//! # Architecture\n28\t//!\n29\t//! ## Scope\n30\t//!\n31\t//! A `Scope` represents a single mapping context with:\n32\t//! - Indentation level (number of leading spaces)\n33\t//! - Set of keys defined within this scope\n34\t//! - Line number where this scope started\n35\t//! - Parent key that created this scope (e.g., \"web\" in \"services: {...}\")\n36\t//! - Whether this scope is in flow-style mapping ({key: value})\n37\t//!\n38\t//! ## ScopeStack\n39\t//!\n40\t//! A `ScopeStack` manages the hierarchical nature of YAML scopes:\n41\t//! - Maintains a stack of active scopes (top = current scope)\n42\t//! - Tracks base indentation size (usually 2 or 4 spaces)\n43\t//! - Handles scope transitions (enter/exit) as indentation changes\n44\t//! - Provides duplicate key detection within proper scope contexts\n45\t//!\n46\t//! # Examples\n47\t//!\n48\t//! ```\n49\t//! use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::{Scope, ScopeStack};\n50\t//!\n51\t//! let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2); // Base indent of 2 spaces\n52\t//!\n53\t//! // Enter a scope when encountering a parent mapping\n54\t//! stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n55\t//!\n56\t//! // Add keys to current scope\n57\t//! stack.add_key(\"web\", 2).unwrap();\n58\t//! stack.add_key(\"database\", 3).unwrap();\n59\t//!\n60\t//! // Detect duplicate in same scope\n61\t//! let result = stack.add_key(\"web\", 4);\n62\t//! assert!(result.is_err()); // Duplicate key error\n63\t//! ```\n64\t\n65\tuse std::collections::HashSet;\n66\tuse std::fmt;\n67\t\n68\t/// A scope representing a mapping context at a specific nesting level\n69\t///\n70\t/// Scopes are created when the parser encounters a parent mapping (a key whose value\n71\t/// is itself a mapping). Each scope maintains its own set of keys, independent of\n72\t/// keys in parent or sibling scopes.\n73\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n74\tpub struct Scope {\n75\t    /// Indentation level (number of leading spaces)\n76\t    pub indent_level: usize,\n77\t    /// Keys defined within this scope\n78\t    pub keys: HashSet<String>,\n79\t    /// Line number where this scope started (1-indexed)\n80\t    pub start_line: usize,\n81\t    /// Parent key that created this scope (e.g., \"web\" in \"services: {...}\")\n82\t    pub parent_key: Option<String>,\n83\t    /// Whether this scope is in flow-style mapping ({key: value})\n84\t    pub is_flow_style: bool,\n85\t}\n86\t\n87\timpl Scope {\n88\t    /// Create a new scope\n89\t    ///\n90\t    /// # Arguments\n91\t    ///\n92\t    /// * `indent_level` - Number of leading spaces for this scope\n93\t    /// * `start_line` - Line number where this scope started (1-indexed)\n94\t    /// * `parent_key` - Optional parent key that created this scope\n95\t    ///\n96\t    /// # Examples\n97\t    ///\n98\t    /// ```\n99\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::Scope;\n100\t    ///\n101\t    /// let scope = Scope::new(2, 5, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n102\t    /// assert_eq!(scope.indent_level, 2);\n103\t    /// assert_eq!(scope.start_line, 5);\n104\t    /// assert_eq!(scope.parent_key, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n105\t    /// ```\n106\t    pub fn new(indent_level: usize, start_line: usize, parent_key: Option<String>) -> Self {\n107\t        Self {\n108\t            indent_level,\n109\t            keys: HashSet::new(),\n110\t            start_line,\n111\t            parent_key,\n112\t            is_flow_style: false,\n113\t        }\n114\t    }\n115\t\n116\t    /// Add a key to this scope, returning true if it's a duplicate\n117\t    ///\n118\t    /// # Arguments\n119\t    ///\n120\t    /// * `key` - The key to add\n121\t    ///\n122\t    /// # Returns\n123\t    ///\n124\t    /// `true` if the key already exists in this scope (duplicate), `false` otherwise\n125\t    ///\n126\t    /// # Examples\n127\t    ///\n128\t    /// ```\n129\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::Scope;\n130\t    ///\n131\t    /// let mut scope = Scope::new(0, 1, None);\n132\t    ///\n133\t    /// assert_eq!(scope.add_key(\"first\"), false); // First occurrence\n134\t    /// assert_eq!(scope.add_key(\"second\"), false); // Different key\n135\t    /// assert_eq!(scope.add_key(\"first\"), true);  // Duplicate!\n136\t    /// ```\n137\t    pub fn add_key(&mut self, key: &str) -> bool {\n138\t        !self.keys.insert(key.to_string())\n139\t    }\n140\t\n141\t    /// Check if this scope contains a key\n142\t    ///\n143\t    /// # Arguments\n144\t    ///\n145\t    /// * `key` - The key to check\n146\t    ///\n147\t    /// # Returns\n148\t    ///\n149\t    /// `true` if the key exists in this scope, `false` otherwise\n150\t    pub fn contains_key(&self, key: &str) -> bool {\n151\t        self.keys.contains(key)\n152\t    }\n153\t\n154\t    /// Get the number of keys in this scope\n155\t    pub fn key_count(&self) -> usize {\n156\t        self.keys.len()\n157\t    }\n158\t\n159\t    /// Clear all keys from this scope\n160\t    pub fn clear_keys(&mut self) {\n161\t        self.keys.clear();\n162\t    }\n163\t}\n164\t\n165\timpl fmt::Display for Scope {\n166\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n167\t        write!(f, \"Scope(indent={}, \", self.indent_level)?;\n168\t        if let Some(ref parent) = self.parent_key {\n169\t            write!(f, \"parent={}, \", parent)?;\n170\t        }\n171\t        write!(f, \"keys={})\", self.keys.len())\n172\t    }\n173\t}\n174\t\n175\t/// Hierarchical stack of active scopes\n176\t///\n177\t/// The ScopeStack maintains the hierarchical nature of YAML scopes during parsing.\n178\t/// As the parser encounters different indentation levels, it enters and exits scopes,\n179\t/// ensuring that duplicate key detection only considers keys within the same scope.\n180\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n181\tpub struct ScopeStack {\n182\t    /// Stack of active scopes (top = current scope)\n183\t    scopes: Vec<Scope>,\n184\t    /// Base indentation size (usually 2 or 4 spaces)\n185\t    base_indent: usize,\n186\t}\n187\t\n188\timpl ScopeStack {\n189\t    /// Create a new scope stack\n190\t    ///\n191\t    /// # Arguments\n192\t    ///\n193\t    /// * `base_indent` - The base indentation size in spaces (usually 2 or 4)\n194\t    ///\n195\t    /// # Examples\n196\t    ///\n197\t    /// ```\n198\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n199\t    ///\n200\t    /// let stack = ScopeStack::new(2); // 2-space indentation\n201\t    /// ```\n202\t    pub fn new(base_indent: usize) -> Self {\n203\t        Self {\n204\t            scopes: vec![Scope::new(0, 0, None)], // Root scope\n205\t            base_indent,\n206\t        }\n207\t    }\n208\t\n209\t    /// Get the current scope (top of stack)\n210\t    ///\n211\t    /// # Panics\n212\t    ///\n213\t    /// Panics if the scope stack is empty (should never happen in normal operation)\n214\t    pub fn current_scope(&mut self) -> &mut Scope {\n215\t        self.scopes.last_mut().expect(\"Scope stack should never be empty\")\n216\t    }\n217\t\n218\t    /// Get the current scope as an immutable reference\n219\t    ///\n220\t    /// # Panics\n221\t    ///\n222\t    /// Panics if the scope stack is empty\n223\t    pub fn current_scope_ref(&self) -> &Scope {\n224\t        self.scopes.last().expect(\"Scope stack should never be empty\")\n225\t    }\n226\t\n227\t    /// Get scope for a specific indentation level\n228\t    ///\n229\t    /// # Arguments\n230\t    ///\n231\t    /// * `indent_level` - The indentation level to search for\n232\t    ///\n233\t    /// # Returns\n234\t    ///\n235\t    /// `Some(&Scope)` if a scope at this level exists, `None` otherwise\n236\t    pub fn get_scope_at_level(&self, indent_level: usize) -> Option<&Scope> {\n237\t        self.scopes.iter().find(|s| s.indent_level == indent_level)\n238\t    }\n239\t\n240\t    /// Enter a new scope (when indent increases)\n241\t    ///\n242\t    /// This method is called when the parser encounters increased indentation,\n243\t    /// indicating entry into a nested scope.\n244\t    ///\n245\t    /// # Arguments\n246\t    ///\n247\t    /// * `indent_level` - The new indentation level\n248\t    /// * `line` - The line number where this scope starts (1-indexed)\n249\t    /// * `parent_key` - Optional parent key that created this scope\n250\t    ///\n251\t    /// # Examples\n252\t    ///\n253\t    /// ```\n254\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n255\t    ///\n256\t    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n257\t    /// stack.enter_scope(2, 5, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n258\t    /// ```\n259\t    pub fn enter_scope(&mut self, indent_level: usize, line: usize, parent_key: Option<String>) {\n260\t        // Check if we already have a scope at this level\n261\t        if let Some(existing) = self.get_scope_at_level(indent_level) {\n262\t            // We're re-entering a scope level - clear and reuse\n263\t            // This handles sibling mappings correctly\n264\t            let mut fresh_scope = Scope::new(indent_level, line, parent_key);\n265\t            fresh_scope.is_flow_style = existing.is_flow_style;\n266\t\n267\t            // Remove all scopes deeper than this level\n268\t            self.scopes.retain(|s| s.indent_level <= indent_level);\n269\t            self.scopes.push(fresh_scope);\n270\t        } else {\n271\t            // Create new scope\n272\t            let new_scope = Scope::new(indent_level, line, parent_key);\n273\t            self.scopes.push(new_scope);\n274\t        }\n275\t    }\n276\t\n277\t    /// Exit to parent scope (when indent decreases)\n278\t    ///\n279\t    /// This method is called when the parser encounters decreased indentation,\n280\t    /// indicating exit from a nested scope back to its parent.\n281\t    ///\n282\t    /// # Arguments\n283\t    ///\n284\t    /// * `target_indent` - The indentation level to exit to\n285\t    ///\n286\t    /// # Examples\n287\t    ///\n288\t    /// ```\n289\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n290\t    ///\n291\t    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n292\t    /// stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"parent\".to_string()));\n293\t    /// stack.exit_to_scope(0); // Exit back to root\n294\t    /// ```\n295\t    pub fn exit_to_scope(&mut self, target_indent: usize) {\n296\t        // Remove all scopes deeper than target\n297\t        self.scopes.retain(|s| s.indent_level <= target_indent);\n298\t\n299\t        // Ensure we have a scope at the target level\n300\t        if !self.scopes.iter().any(|s| s.indent_level == target_indent) {\n301\t            // This shouldn't happen in valid YAML, but handle gracefully\n302\t            let fallback_scope = Scope::new(target_indent, 0, None);\n303\t            self.scopes.push(fallback_scope);\n304\t        }\n305\t    }\n306\t\n307\t    /// Check if current scope contains a key\n308\t    ///\n309\t    /// # Arguments\n310\t    ///\n311\t    /// * `key` - The key to check for\n312\t    ///\n313\t    /// # Returns\n314\t    ///\n315\t    /// `true` if the key exists in the current scope, `false` otherwise\n316\t    pub fn contains_key(&self, key: &str) -> bool {\n317\t        self.scopes.last()\n318\t            .and_then(|scope| Some(scope.contains_key(key)))\n319\t            .unwrap_or(false)\n320\t    }\n321\t\n322\t    /// Check if any scope in the hierarchy contains a key\n323\t    ///\n324\t    /// This searches through all scopes in the stack, not just the current one.\n325\t    ///\n326\t    /// # Arguments\n327\t    ///\n328\t    /// * `key` - The key to search for\n329\t    ///\n330\t    /// # Returns\n331\t    ///\n332\t    /// `true` if the key exists in any scope, `false` otherwise\n333\t    pub fn contains_key_in_any_scope(&self, key: &str) -> bool {\n334\t        self.scopes.iter().any(|scope| scope.contains_key(key))\n335\t    }\n336\t\n337\t    /// Add a key to current scope\n338\t    ///\n339\t    /// # Arguments\n340\t    ///\n341\t    /// * `key` - The key to add\n342\t    /// * `line` - The line number where the key appears (1-indexed)\n343\t    ///\n344\t    /// # Returns\n345\t    ///\n346\t    /// `Ok(())` if the key was added successfully\n347\t    /// `Err(DuplicateKeyError)` if the key already exists in the current scope\n348\t    ///\n349\t    /// # Examples\n350\t    ///\n351\t    /// ```\n352\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n353\t    ///\n354\t    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n355\t    ///\n356\t    /// // First occurrence succeeds\n357\t    /// assert!(stack.add_key(\"host\", 1).is_ok());\n358\t    ///\n359\t    /// // Second occurrence fails\n360\t    /// assert!(stack.add_key(\"host\", 2).is_err());\n361\t    /// ```\n362\t    pub fn add_key(&mut self, key: &str, line: usize) -> Result<(), DuplicateKeyError> {\n363\t        let scope_path = self.get_scope_path();\n364\t\n365\t        // Check if key exists before adding\n366\t        if self.contains_key(key) {\n367\t            let scope = self.current_scope();\n368\t            Err(DuplicateKeyError {\n369\t                key: key.to_string(),\n370\t                scope_path,\n371\t                first_line: scope.start_line,\n372\t                duplicate_line: line,\n373\t            })\n374\t        } else {\n375\t            let scope = self.current_scope();\n376\t            scope.add_key(key);\n377\t            Ok(())\n378\t        }\n379\t    }\n380\t\n381\t    /// Get human-readable path to current scope\n382\t    ///\n383\t    /// Returns a dot-separated path representing the scope hierarchy,\n384\t    /// e.g., \"services.web.database\" for a deeply nested scope.\n385\t    ///\n386\t    /// # Returns\n387\t    ///\n388\t    /// A string representing the scope path\n389\t    ///\n390\t    /// # Examples\n391\t    ///\n392\t    /// ```\n393\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n394\t    ///\n395\t    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n396\t    /// stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n397\t    /// stack.enter_scope(4, 2, Some(\"web\".to_string()));\n398\t    /// assert_eq!(stack.get_scope_path(), \"services.web\");\n399\t    /// ```\n400\t    pub fn get_scope_path(&self) -> String {\n401\t        let mut path = Vec::new();\n402\t        for scope in &self.scopes {\n403\t            if let Some(ref key) = scope.parent_key {\n404\t                path.push(key.clone());\n405\t            }\n406\t        }\n407\t        path.join(\".\")\n408\t    }\n409\t\n410\t    /// Get current indent level\n411\t    ///\n412\t    /// # Returns\n413\t    ///\n414\t    /// The indentation level of the current scope\n415\t    pub fn current_indent(&self) -> usize {\n416\t        self.scopes.last()\n417\t            .map(|scope| scope.indent_level)\n418\t            .unwrap_or(0)\n419\t    }\n420\t\n421\t    /// Get the number of active scopes in the stack\n422\t    pub fn depth(&self) -> usize {\n423\t        self.scopes.len()\n424\t    }\n425\t\n426\t    /// Clear all scopes and reset to root\n427\t    ///\n428\t    /// This resets the scope stack to its initial state with only the root scope.\n429\t    pub fn reset(&mut self) {\n430\t        self.scopes = vec![Scope::new(0, 0, None)];\n431\t    }\n432\t\n433\t    /// Get the base indentation size\n434\t    pub fn base_indent(&self) -> usize {\n435\t        self.base_indent\n436\t    }\n437\t\n438\t    /// Set the base indentation size\n439\t    ///\n440\t    /// # Arguments\n441\t    ///\n442\t    /// * `base_indent` - The new base indentation size in spaces\n443\t    pub fn set_base_indent(&mut self, base_indent: usize) {\n444\t        self.base_indent = base_indent;\n445\t    }\n446\t}\n447\t\n448\timpl fmt::Display for ScopeStack {\n449\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n450\t        write!(f, \"ScopeStack(depth={}, base_indent={}, current_path={})\",\n451\t               self.depth(), self.base_indent, self.get_scope_path())\n452\t    }\n453\t}\n454\t\n455\t/// Error when a duplicate key is detected\n456\t///\n457\t/// This error is returned when attempting to add a key that already exists\n458\t/// in the current scope.\n459\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n460\tpub struct DuplicateKeyError {\n461\t    /// The duplicate key\n462\t    pub key: String,\n463\t    /// The scope path where the duplicate was found\n464\t    pub scope_path: String,\n465\t    /// The line number where the key was first defined\n466\t    pub first_line: usize,\n467\t    /// The line number where the duplicate was found\n468\t    pub duplicate_line: usize,\n469\t}\n470\t\n471\timpl DuplicateKeyError {\n472\t    /// Create a new duplicate key error\n473\t    ///\n474\t    /// # Arguments\n475\t    ///\n476\t    /// * `key` - The duplicate key name\n477\t    /// * `scope_path` - The scope path where the duplicate was found\n478\t    /// * `first_line` - Line number of first occurrence\n479\t    /// * `duplicate_line` - Line number of duplicate occurrence\n480\t    pub fn new(key: String, scope_path: String, first_line: usize, duplicate_line: usize) -> Self {\n481\t        Self {\n482\t            key,\n483\t            scope_path,\n484\t            first_line,\n485\t            duplicate_line,\n486\t        }\n487\t    }\n488\t\n489\t    /// Get a formatted error message\n490\t    ///\n491\t    /// # Returns\n492\t    ///\n493\t    /// A human-readable error message\n494\t    pub fn message(&self) -> String {\n495\t        format!(\n496\t            \"Line {}: duplicate key '{}' in scope '{}'\\n  First defined at: Line {}\",\n497\t            self.duplicate_line, self.key, self.scope_path, self.first_line\n498\t        )\n499\t    }\n500\t}\n501\t\n502\timpl fmt::Display for DuplicateKeyError {\n503\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n504\t        write!(\n505\t            f,\n506\t            \"Line {}: duplicate key '{}' in scope '{}'\\n  First defined at: Line {}\",\n507\t            self.duplicate_line, self.key, self.scope_path, self.first_line\n508\t        )\n509\t    }\n510\t}\n511\t\n512\timpl std::error::Error for DuplicateKeyError {}\n513\t\n514\t/// Key context classification\n515\t///\n516\t/// Enumerates the different contexts in which a key can appear in a YAML document.\n517\t/// This classification helps the parser understand how to handle different key types.\n518\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]\n519\tpub enum KeyContext {\n520\t    /// Key with inline scalar value: \"key: value\"\n521\t    ///\n522\t    /// The key has a simple scalar value on the same line\n523\t    InlineScalar {\n524\t        /// The key name\n525\t        key: String,\n526\t        /// The scalar value\n527\t        value: String,\n528\t    },\n529\t\n530\t    /// Parent key with nested mapping: \"key:\\n  nested: value\"\n531\t    ///\n532\t    /// The key's value is a nested mapping (indentation increases after this key)\n533\t    ParentMapping {\n534\t        /// The parent key name\n535\t        key: String,\n536\t    },\n537\t\n538\t    /// Key with nested sequence: \"key:\\n  - item1\"\n539\t    ///\n540\t    /// The key's value is a sequence of items\n541\t    ParentSequence {\n542\t        /// The parent key name\n543\t        key: String,\n544\t    },\n545\t}\n546\t\n547\timpl KeyContext {\n548\t    /// Get the key name from any key context variant\n549\t    ///\n550\t    /// # Returns\n551\t    ///\n552\t    /// The key name as a string slice\n553\t    pub fn key_name(&self) -> &str {\n554\t        match self {\n555\t            KeyContext::InlineScalar { key, .. } => key,\n556\t            KeyContext::ParentMapping { key } => key,\n557\t            KeyContext::ParentSequence { key } => key,\n558\t        }\n559\t    }\n560\t\n561\t    /// Check if this is a parent key (creates a new scope)\n562\t    ///\n563\t    /// # Returns\n564\t    ///\n565\t    /// `true` if this key creates a new scope (mapping or sequence)\n566\t    pub fn is_parent_key(&self) -> bool {\n567\t        matches!(self, KeyContext::ParentMapping { .. } | KeyContext::ParentSequence { .. })\n568\t    }\n569\t\n570\t    /// Check if this is an inline scalar (does not create a new scope)\n571\t    ///\n572\t    /// # Returns\n573\t    ///\n574\t    /// `true` if this key has an inline scalar value\n575\t    pub fn is_inline_scalar(&self) -> bool {\n576\t        matches!(self, KeyContext::InlineScalar { .. })\n577\t    }\n578\t}\n579\t\n580\timpl fmt::Display for KeyContext {\n581\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n582\t        match self {\n583\t            KeyContext::InlineScalar { key, value } => {\n584\t                write!(f, \"InlineScalar(key='{}', value='{}')\", key, value)\n585\t            }\n586\t            KeyContext::ParentMapping { key } => {\n587\t                write!(f, \"ParentMapping(key='{}')\", key)\n588\t            }\n589\t            KeyContext::ParentSequence { key } => {\n590\t                write!(f, \"ParentSequence(key='{}')\", key)\n591\t            }\n592\t        }\n593\t    }\n594\t}\n595\t\n596\t/// Extract key context from a line\n597\t///\n598\t/// Analyzes a YAML line to determine the context of a key.\n599\t/// Returns `None` if the line doesn't contain a valid key.\n600\t///\n601\t/// # Arguments\n602\t///\n603\t/// * `line` - The YAML line to analyze\n604\t///\n605\t/// # Returns\n606\t///\n607\t/// `Some(KeyContext)` if a key is found, `None` otherwise\n608\t///\n609\t/// # Examples\n610\t///\n611\t/// ```\n612\t/// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::{extract_key_context, KeyContext};\n613\t///\n614\t/// // Inline scalar\n615\t/// let ctx = extract_key_context(\"host: localhost\").unwrap();\n616\t/// assert!(matches!(ctx, KeyContext::InlineScalar { .. }));\n617\t///\n618\t/// // Parent mapping\n619\t/// let ctx = extract_key_context(\"services:\").unwrap();\n620\t/// assert!(matches!(ctx, KeyContext::ParentMapping { .. }));\n621\t/// ```\n622\tpub fn extract_key_context(line: &str) -> Option<KeyContext> {\n623\t    let trimmed = line.trim();\n624\t\n625\t    // Find colon position\n626\t    let colon_pos = trimmed.find(':')?;\n627\t    let key_part = &trimmed[..colon_pos];\n628\t    let after_colon = &trimmed[colon_pos + 1..];\n629\t\n630\t    // Skip if key is empty\n631\t    let key = key_part.trim();\n632\t    if key.is_empty() {\n633\t        return None;\n634\t    }\n635\t\n636\t    // Skip if key contains invalid characters (like in flow style)\n637\t    if key.contains('{') || key.contains('}') || key.contains('[') || key.contains(']') {\n638\t        return None;\n639\t    }\n640\t\n641\t    // Classify based on what comes after the colon\n642\t    let context = if after_colon.trim().is_empty() {\n643\t        KeyContext::ParentMapping { key: key.to_string() }\n644\t    } else {\n645\t        KeyContext::InlineScalar {\n646\t            key: key.to_string(),\n647\t            value: after_colon.trim().to_string(),\n648\t        }\n649\t    };\n650\t\n651\t    Some(context)\n652\t}\n653\t\n654\t/// Get leading whitespace length from a line\n655\t///\n656\t/// # Arguments\n657\t///\n658\t/// * `line` - The line to analyze\n659\t///\n660\t/// # Returns\n661\t///\n662\t/// The number of leading whitespace characters\n663\t///\n664\t/// # Examples\n665\t///\n666\t/// ```\n667\t/// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::get_leading_whitespace_length;\n668\t///\n669\t/// assert_eq!(get_leading_whitespace_length(\"  key: value\"), 2);\n670\t/// assert_eq!(get_leading_whitespace_length(\"key: value\"), 0);\n671\t/// assert_eq!(get_leading_whitespace_length(\"    nested: value\"), 4);\n672\t/// ```\n673\tpub fn get_leading_whitespace_length(line: &str) -> usize {\n674\t    line.chars().take_while(|c| c.is_whitespace()).count()\n675\t}\n676\t\n677\t#[cfg(test)]\n678\tmod tests {\n679\t    use super::*;\n680\t\n681\t    #[test]\n682\t    fn test_scope_creation() {\n683\t        let scope = Scope::new(2, 5, Some(\"parent\".to_string()));\n684\t        assert_eq!(scope.indent_level,
