}\n877\t}\n878\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"920a98d5-4f35-4e6d-a665-fa07c0507566","uuid":"3fb2c114-91a7-4a93-89c7-d62e4ba69c38","timestamp":"2026-07-13T18:32:17.615Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/ARMOR/src/parsers/yaml/scope.rs","content":"//! Scope representation data structures\n//!\n//! This module defines the core data structures for representing hierarchical scopes\n//! in YAML documents. These structures enable accurate duplicate key detection by\n//! tracking keys within their proper scope contexts.\n//!\n//! # Overview\n//!\n//! YAML documents use indentation to define nested scopes. Keys in different scopes\n//! can have the same name without being duplicates. For example:\n//!\n//! ```yaml\n//! services:\n//!   web:\n//!     host: localhost    # \"host\" in services.web scope\n//!     port: 8080\n//!   database:\n//!     host: db.example.com  # \"host\" in services.database scope (NOT a duplicate)\n//!     port: 5432\n//! ```\n//!\n//! The scope representation system tracks:\n//! - **Scope**: A mapping context at a specific nesting level\n//! - **ScopeStack**: A hierarchical stack of active scopes during parsing\n//! - **KeyContext**: Classification of key types (inline scalar, parent mapping, parent sequence)\n//!\n//! # Architecture\n//!\n//! ## Scope\n//!\n//! A `Scope` represents a single mapping context with:\n//! - Indentation level (number of leading spaces)\n//! - Set of keys defined within this scope\n//! - Line number where this scope started\n//! - Parent key that created this scope (e.g., \"web\" in \"services: {...}\")\n//! - Whether this scope is in flow-style mapping ({key: value})\n//!\n//! ## ScopeStack\n//!\n//! A `ScopeStack` manages the hierarchical nature of YAML scopes:\n//! - Maintains a stack of active scopes (top = current scope)\n//! - Tracks base indentation size (usually 2 or 4 spaces)\n//! - Handles scope transitions (enter/exit) as indentation changes\n//! - Provides duplicate key detection within proper scope contexts\n//!\n//! # Examples\n//!\n//! ```\n//! use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::{Scope, ScopeStack};\n//!\n//! let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2); // Base indent of 2 spaces\n//!\n//! // Enter a scope when encountering a parent mapping\n//! stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n//!\n//! // Add keys to current scope\n//! stack.add_key(\"web\", 2).unwrap();\n//! stack.add_key(\"database\", 3).unwrap();\n//!\n//! // Detect duplicate in same scope\n//! let result = stack.add_key(\"web\", 4);\n//! assert!(result.is_err()); // Duplicate key error\n//! ```\n\nuse std::collections::HashSet;\nuse std::fmt;\n\n/// A scope representing a mapping context at a specific nesting level\n///\n/// Scopes are created when the parser encounters a parent mapping (a key whose value\n/// is itself a mapping). Each scope maintains its own set of keys, independent of\n/// keys in parent or sibling scopes.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct Scope {\n    /// Indentation level (number of leading spaces)\n    pub indent_level: usize,\n    /// Keys defined within this scope\n    pub keys: HashSet<String>,\n    /// Line number where this scope started (1-indexed)\n    pub start_line: usize,\n    /// Parent key that created this scope (e.g., \"web\" in \"services: {...}\")\n    pub parent_key: Option<String>,\n    /// Whether this scope is in flow-style mapping ({key: value})\n    pub is_flow_style: bool,\n}\n\nimpl Scope {\n    /// Create a new scope\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `indent_level` - Number of leading spaces for this scope\n    /// * `start_line` - Line number where this scope started (1-indexed)\n    /// * `parent_key` - Optional parent key that created this scope\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::Scope;\n    ///\n    /// let scope = Scope::new(2, 5, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n    /// assert_eq!(scope.indent_level, 2);\n    /// assert_eq!(scope.start_line, 5);\n    /// assert_eq!(scope.parent_key, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n    /// ```\n    pub fn new(indent_level: usize, start_line: usize, parent_key: Option<String>) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            indent_level,\n            keys: HashSet::new(),\n            start_line,\n            parent_key,\n            is_flow_style: false,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Add a key to this scope, returning true if it's a duplicate\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `key` - The key to add\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// `true` if the key already exists in this scope (duplicate), `false` otherwise\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::Scope;\n    ///\n    /// let mut scope = Scope::new(0, 1, None);\n    ///\n    /// assert_eq!(scope.add_key(\"first\"), false); // First occurrence\n    /// assert_eq!(scope.add_key(\"second\"), false); // Different key\n    /// assert_eq!(scope.add_key(\"first\"), true);  // Duplicate!\n    /// ```\n    pub fn add_key(&mut self, key: &str) -> bool {\n        !self.keys.insert(key.to_string())\n    }\n\n    /// Check if this scope contains a key\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `key` - The key to check\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// `true` if the key exists in this scope, `false` otherwise\n    pub fn contains_key(&self, key: &str) -> bool {\n        self.keys.contains(key)\n    }\n\n    /// Get the number of keys in this scope\n    pub fn key_count(&self) -> usize {\n        self.keys.len()\n    }\n\n    /// Clear all keys from this scope\n    pub fn clear_keys(&mut self) {\n        self.keys.clear();\n    }\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Display for Scope {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        write!(f, \"Scope(indent={}, \", self.indent_level)?;\n        if let Some(ref parent) = self.parent_key {\n            write!(f, \"parent={}, \", parent)?;\n        }\n        write!(f, \"keys={})\", self.keys.len())\n    }\n}\n\n/// Hierarchical stack of active scopes\n///\n/// The ScopeStack maintains the hierarchical nature of YAML scopes during parsing.\n/// As the parser encounters different indentation levels, it enters and exits scopes,\n/// ensuring that duplicate key detection only considers keys within the same scope.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct ScopeStack {\n    /// Stack of active scopes (top = current scope)\n    scopes: Vec<Scope>,\n    /// Base indentation size (usually 2 or 4 spaces)\n    base_indent: usize,\n}\n\nimpl ScopeStack {\n    /// Create a new scope stack\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `base_indent` - The base indentation size in spaces (usually 2 or 4)\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n    ///\n    /// let stack = ScopeStack::new(2); // 2-space indentation\n    /// ```\n    pub fn new(base_indent: usize) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            scopes: vec![Scope::new(0, 0, None)], // Root scope\n            base_indent,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get the current scope (top of stack)\n    ///\n    /// # Panics\n    ///\n    /// Panics if the scope stack is empty (should never happen in normal operation)\n    pub fn current_scope(&mut self) -> &mut Scope {\n        self.scopes.last_mut().expect(\"Scope stack should never be empty\")\n    }\n\n    /// Get the current scope as an immutable reference\n    ///\n    /// # Panics\n    ///\n    /// Panics if the scope stack is empty\n    pub fn current_scope_ref(&self) -> &Scope {\n        self.scopes.last().expect(\"Scope stack should never be empty\")\n    }\n\n    /// Get scope for a specific indentation level\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `indent_level` - The indentation level to search for\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// `Some(&Scope)` if a scope at this level exists, `None` otherwise\n    pub fn get_scope_at_level(&self, indent_level: usize) -> Option<&Scope> {\n        self.scopes.iter().find(|s| s.indent_level == indent_level)\n    }\n\n    /// Enter a new scope (when indent increases)\n    ///\n    /// This method is called when the parser encounters increased indentation,\n    /// indicating entry into a nested scope.\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `indent_level` - The new indentation level\n    /// * `line` - The line number where this scope starts (1-indexed)\n    /// * `parent_key` - Optional parent key that created this scope\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n    ///\n    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n    /// stack.enter_scope(2, 5, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n    /// ```\n    pub fn enter_scope(&mut self, indent_level: usize, line: usize, parent_key: Option<String>) {\n        // Check if we already have a scope at this level\n        if let Some(existing) = self.get_scope_at_level(indent_level) {\n            // We're re-entering a scope level - clear and reuse\n            // This handles sibling mappings correctly\n            let mut fresh_scope = Scope::new(indent_level, line, parent_key);\n            fresh_scope.is_flow_style = existing.is_flow_style;\n\n            // Remove all scopes deeper than this level\n            self.scopes.retain(|s| s.indent_level <= indent_level);\n            self.scopes.push(fresh_scope);\n        } else {\n            // Create new scope\n            let new_scope = Scope::new(indent_level, line, parent_key);\n            self.scopes.push(new_scope);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Exit to parent scope (when indent decreases)\n    ///\n    /// This method is called when the parser encounters decreased indentation,\n    /// indicating exit from a nested scope back to its parent.\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `target_indent` - The indentation level to exit to\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n    ///\n    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n    /// stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"parent\".to_string()));\n    /// stack.exit_to_scope(0); // Exit back to root\n    /// ```\n    pub fn exit_to_scope(&mut self, target_indent: usize) {\n        // Remove all scopes deeper than target\n        self.scopes.retain(|s| s.indent_level <= target_indent);\n\n        // Ensure we have a scope at the target level\n        if !self.scopes.iter().any(|s| s.indent_level == target_indent) {\n            // This shouldn't happen in valid YAML, but handle gracefully\n            let fallback_scope = Scope::new(target_indent, 0, None);\n            self.scopes.push(fallback_scope);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check if current scope contains a key\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `key` - The key to check for\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// `true` if the key exists in the current scope, `false` otherwise\n    pub fn contains_key(&self, key: &str) -> bool {\n        self.scopes.last()\n            .and_then(|scope| Some(scope.contains_key(key)))\n            .unwrap_or(false)\n    }\n\n    /// Check if any scope in the hierarchy contains a key\n    ///\n    /// This searches through all scopes in the stack, not just the current one.\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `key` - The key to search for\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// `true` if the key exists in any scope, `false` otherwise\n    pub fn contains_key_in_any_scope(&self, key: &str) -> bool {\n        self.scopes.iter().any(|scope| scope.contains_key(key))\n    }\n\n    /// Add a key to current scope\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `key` - The key to add\n    /// * `line` - The line number where the key appears (1-indexed)\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// `Ok(())` if the key was added successfully\n    /// `Err(DuplicateKeyError)` if the key already exists in the current scope\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n    ///\n    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n    ///\n    /// // First occurrence succeeds\n    /// assert!(stack.add_key(\"host\", 1).is_ok());\n    ///\n    /// // Second occurrence fails\n    /// assert!(stack.add_key(\"host\", 2).is_err());\n    /// ```\n    pub fn add_key(&mut self, key: &str, line: usize) -> Result<(), DuplicateKeyError> {\n        let scope_path = self.get_scope_path();\n\n        // Check if key exists before adding\n        if self.contains_key(key) {\n            let scope = self.current_scope();\n            Err(DuplicateKeyError {\n                key: key.to_string(),\n                scope_path,\n                first_line: scope.start_line,\n                duplicate_line: line,\n            })\n        } else {\n            let scope = self.current_scope();\n            scope.add_key(key);\n            Ok(())\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get human-readable path to current scope\n    ///\n    /// Returns a dot-separated path representing the scope hierarchy,\n    /// e.g., \"services.web.database\" for a deeply nested scope.\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// A string representing the scope path\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n    ///\n    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n    /// stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n    /// stack.enter_scope(4, 2, Some(\"web\".to_string()));\n    /// assert_eq!(stack.get_scope_path(), \"services.web\");\n    /// ```\n    pub fn get_scope_path(&self) -> String {\n        let mut path = Vec::new();\n        for scope in &self.scopes {\n            if let Some(ref key) = scope.parent_key {\n                path.push(key.clone());\n            }\n        }\n        path.join(\".\")\n    }\n\n    /// Get current indent level\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// The indentation level of the current scope\n    pub fn current_indent(&self) -> usize {\n        self.scopes.last()\n            .map(|scope| scope.indent_level)\n            .unwrap_or(0)\n    }\n\n    /// Get the number of active scopes in the stack\n    pub fn depth(&self) -> usize {\n        self.scopes.len()\n    }\n\n    /// Clear all scopes and reset to root\n    ///\n    /// This resets the scope stack to its initial state with only the root scope.\n    pub fn reset(&mut self) {\n        self.scopes = vec![Scope::new(0, 0, None)];\n    }\n\n    /// Get the base indentation size\n    pub fn base_indent(&self) -> usize {\n        self.base_indent\n    }\n\n    /// Set the base indentation size\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `base_indent` - The new base indentation size in spaces\n    pub fn set_base_indent(&mut self, base_indent: usize) {\n        self.base_indent = base_indent;\n    }\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Display for ScopeStack {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        write!(f, \"ScopeStack(depth={}, base_indent={}, current_path={})\",\n               self.depth(), self.base_indent, self.get_scope_path())\n    }\n}\n\n/// Error when a duplicate key is detected\n///\n/// This error is returned when attempting to add a key that already exists\n/// in the current scope.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct DuplicateKeyError {\n    /// The duplicate key\n    pub key: String,\n    /// The scope path where the duplicate was found\n    pub scope_path: String,\n    /// The line number where the key was first defined\n    pub first_line: usize,\n    /// The line number where the duplicate was found\n    pub duplicate_line: usize,\n}\n\nimpl DuplicateKeyError {\n    /// Create a new duplicate key error\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `key` - The duplicate key name\n    /// * `scope_path` - The scope path where the duplicate was found\n    /// * `first_line` - Line number of first occurrence\n    /// * `duplicate_line` - Line number of duplicate occurrence\n    pub fn new(key: String, scope_path: String, first_line: usize, duplicate_line: usize) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            key,\n            scope_path,\n            first_line,\n            duplicate_line,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get a formatted error message\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// A human-readable error message\n    pub fn message(&self) -> String {\n        format!(\n            \"Line {}: duplicate key '{}' in scope '{}'\\n  First defined at: Line {}\",\n            self.duplicate_line, self.key, self.scope_path, self.first_line\n        )\n    }\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Display for DuplicateKeyError {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        write!(\n            f,\n            \"Line {}: duplicate key '{}' in scope '{}'\\n  First defined at: Line {}\",\n            self.duplicate_line, self.key, self.scope_path, self.first_line\n        )\n    }\n}\n\nimpl std::error::Error for DuplicateKeyError {}\n\n/// Key context classification\n///\n/// Enumerates the different contexts in which a key can appear in a YAML document.\n/// This classification helps the parser understand how to handle different key types.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]\npub enum KeyContext {\n    /// Key with inline scalar value: \"key: value\"\n    ///\n    /// The key has a simple scalar value on the same line\n    InlineScalar {\n        /// The key name\n        key: String,\n        /// The scalar value\n        value: String,\n    },\n\n    /// Parent key with nested mapping: \"key:\\n  nested: value\"\n    ///\n    /// The key's value is a nested mapping (indentation increases after this key)\n    ParentMapping {\n        /// The parent key name\n        key: String,\n    },\n\n    /// Key with nested sequence: \"key:\\n  - item1\"\n    ///\n    /// The key's value is a sequence of items\n    ParentSequence {\n        /// The parent key name\n        key: String,\n    },\n}\n\nimpl KeyContext {\n    /// Get the key name from any key context variant\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// The key name as a string slice\n    pub fn key_name(&self) -> &str {\n        match self {\n            KeyContext::InlineScalar { key, .. } => key,\n            KeyContext::ParentMapping { key } => key,\n            KeyContext::ParentSequence { key } => key,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check if this is a parent key (creates a new scope)\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// `true` if this key creates a new scope (mapping or sequence)\n    pub fn is_parent_key(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, KeyContext::ParentMapping { .. } | KeyContext::ParentSequence { .. })\n    }\n\n    /// Check if this is an inline scalar (does not create a new scope)\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// `true` if this key has an inline scalar value\n    pub fn is_inline_scalar(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, KeyContext::InlineScalar { .. })\n    }\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Display for KeyContext {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        match self {\n            KeyContext::InlineScalar { key, value } => {\n                write!(f, \"InlineScalar(key='{}', value='{}')\", key, value)\n            }\n            KeyContext::ParentMapping { key } => {\n                write!(f, \"ParentMapping(key='{}')\", key)\n            }\n            KeyContext::ParentSequence { key } => {\n                write!(f, \"ParentSequence(key='{}')\", key)\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Extract key context from a line\n///\n/// Analyzes a YAML line to determine the context of a key.\n/// Returns `None` if the line doesn't contain a valid key.\n///\n/// # Arguments\n///\n/// * `line` - The YAML line to analyze\n///\n/// # Returns\n///\n/// `Some(KeyContext)` if a key is found, `None` otherwise\n///\n/// # Examples\n///\n/// ```\n/// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::{extract_key_context, KeyContext};\n///\n/// // Inline scalar\n/// let ctx = extract_key_context(\"host: localhost\").unwrap();\n/// assert!(matches!(ctx, KeyContext::InlineScalar { .. }));\n///\n/// // Parent mapping\n/// let ctx = extract_key_context(\"services:\").unwrap();\n/// assert!(matches!(ctx, KeyContext::ParentMapping { .. }));\n/// ```\npub fn extract_key_context(line: &str) -> Option<KeyContext> {\n    let trimmed = line.trim();\n\n    // Find colon position\n    let colon_pos = trimmed.find(':')?;\n    let key_part = &trimmed[..colon_pos];\n    let after_colon = &trimmed[colon_pos + 1..];\n\n    // Skip if key is empty\n    let key = key_part.trim();\n    if key.is_empty() {\n        return None;\n    }\n\n    // Skip if key contains invalid characters (like in flow style)\n    if key.contains('{') || key.contains('}') || key.contains('[') || key.contains(']') {\n        return None;\n    }\n\n    // Classify based on what comes after the colon\n    let context = if after_colon.trim().is_empty() {\n        KeyContext::ParentMapping { key: key.to_string() }\n    } else {\n        KeyContext::InlineScalar {\n            key: key.to_string(),\n            value: after_colon.trim().to_string(),\n        }\n    };\n\n    Some(context)\n}\n\n/// Get leading whitespace length from a line\n///\n/// # Arguments\n///\n/// * `line` - The line to analyze\n///\n/// # Returns\n///\n/// The number of leading whitespace characters\n///\n/// # Examples\n///\n/// ```\n/// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::get_leading_whitespace_length;\n///\n/// assert_eq!(get_leading_whitespace_length(\"  key: value\"), 2);\n/// assert_eq!(get_leading_whitespace_length(\"key: value\"), 0);\n/// assert_eq!(get_leading_whitespace_length(\"    nested: value\"), 4);\n/// ```\npub fn get_leading_whitespace_length(line: &str) -> usize {\n    line.chars().take_while(|c| c.is_whitespace()).count()\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_scope_creation() {\n        let scope = Scope::new(2, 5, Some(\"parent\".to_string()));\n        assert_eq!(scope.indent_level, 2);\n        assert_eq!(scope.start_line, 5);\n        assert_eq!(scope.parent_key, Some(\"parent\".to_string()));\n        assert_eq!(scope.key_count(), 0);\n        assert!(!scope.is_flow_style);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_scope_add_key() {\n        let mut scope = Scope::new(0, 1, None);\n\n        assert_eq!(scope.add_key(\"first\"), false);\n        assert_eq!(scope.add_key(\"second\"), false);\n        assert_eq!(scope.key_count(), 2);\n\n        // Duplicate detection\n        assert_eq!(scope.add_key(\"first\"), true);\n        assert_eq!(scope.key_count(), 2); // Not added\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_scope_contains_key() {\n        let mut scope = Scope::new(0, 1, None);\n\n        assert!(!scope.contains_key(\"test\"));\n        scope.add_key(\"test\");\n        assert!(scope.contains_key(\"test\"));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_scope_stack_creation() {\n        let stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n        assert_eq!(stack.base_indent(), 2);\n        assert_eq!(stack.depth(), 1); // Root scope\n        assert_eq!(stack.current_indent(), 0);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_scope_stack_enter_exit() {\n        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n        stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"parent\".to_string()));\n        assert_eq!(stack.depth(), 2);\n        assert_eq!(stack.current_indent(), 2);\n        assert_eq!(stack.get_scope_path(), \"parent\");\n\n        stack.enter_scope(4, 2, Some(\"child\".to_string()));\n        assert_eq!(stack.depth(), 3);\n        assert_eq!(stack.get_scope_path(), \"parent.child\");\n\n        stack.exit_to_scope(2);\n        assert_eq!(stack.depth(), 2);\n        assert_eq!(stack.current_indent(), 2);\n        assert_eq!(stack.get_scope_path(), \"parent\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_scope_stack_add_key() {\n        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n        // Add to root scope\n        assert!(stack.add_key(\"root_key\", 1).is_ok());\n        assert!(stack.contains_key(\"root_key\"));\n\n        // Duplicate detection in same scope\n        assert!(stack.add_key(\"root_key\", 2).is_err());\n\n        // Different key succeeds\n        assert!(stack.add_key(\"another_key\", 3).is_ok());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_scope_stack_add_key_in_nested_scope() {\n        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n        // Add to root\n        stack.add_key(\"host\", 1).unwrap();\n\n        // Enter nested scope\n        stack.enter_scope(2, 2, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n\n        // Same key name is OK in different scope\n        assert!(stack.add_key(\"host\", 3).is_ok());\n        assert!(stack.contains_key(\"host\"));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_scope_stack_reset() {\n        let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n        stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"parent\".to_string()));\n        stack.add_key(\"key\", 2).unwrap();\n\n        assert_eq!(stack.depth(), 2);\n\n        stack.reset();\n\n        assert_eq!(stack.depth(), 1);\n        assert!(!stack.contains_key(\"key\"));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_duplicate_key_error() {\n        let error = DuplicateKeyError::new(\n            \"host\".to_string(),\n            \"services.web\".to_string(),\n            5,\n            10,\n        );\n\n        assert_eq!(error.key, \"host\");\n        assert_eq!(error.scope_path, \"services.web\");\n        assert_eq!(error.first_line, 5);\n        assert_eq!(error.duplicate_line, 10);\n\n        let msg = error.message();\n        assert!(msg.contains(\"duplicate key\"));\n        assert!(msg.contains(\"host\"));\n        assert!(msg.contains(\"services.web\"));\n        assert!(msg.contains(\"Line 10\"));\n        assert!(msg.contains(\"Line 5\"));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_key_context_inline_scalar() {\n        let ctx = extract_key_context(\"host: localhost\").unwrap();\n        assert!(matches!(ctx, KeyContext::InlineScalar { .. }));\n        assert_eq!(ctx.key_name(), \"host\");\n        assert!(ctx.is_inline_scalar());\n        assert!(!ctx.is_parent_key());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_key_context_parent_mapping() {\n        let ctx = extract_key_context(\"services:\").unwrap();\n        assert!(matches!(ctx, KeyContext::ParentMapping { .. }));\n        assert_eq!(ctx.key_name(), \"services\");\n        assert!(!ctx.is_inline_scalar());\n        assert!(ctx.is_parent_key());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_extract_key_context_invalid() {\n        // No colon\n        assert!(extract_key_context(\"no key here\").is_none());\n\n        // Empty key\n        assert!(extract_key_context(\": value\").is_none());\n\n        // Flow style (contains braces)\n        assert!(extract_key_context(\"{key: value}\").is_none());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_get_leading_whitespace_length() {\n        assert_eq!(get_leading_whitespace_length(\"key: value\"), 0);\n        assert_eq!(get_leading_whitespace_length(\"  key: value\"), 2);\n        assert_eq!(get_leading_whitespace_length(\"    key: value\"), 4);\n        assert_eq!(get_leading_whitespace_length(\"\\tkey: value\"), 1); // Tab counts as 1\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_scope_stack_sibling_mappings() {\n        let mut       // Second sibling at same level\n        stack.enter_scope(2, 5, Some(\"database\".to_string()));\n        // Same key should be OK (different scope)\n        assert!(stack.add_key(\"host\", This struct orchestrates all syntax detection checks and provides\n184\t/// a unified interface for error detection.\n185\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n186\tpub struct SyntaxDetector {\n187\t    /// Configuration for detection behavior\n188\t    config: DetectorConfig,\n189\t    /// Track indentation state across lines\n190\t    indentation_state: IndentationState,\n191\t    /// Track delimiter state (brackets, braces, quotes)\n192\t    delimiter_state: DelimiterState,\n193\t    /// Track structure state (keys, sequences, mappings)\n194\t    structure_state: mixing\n201\t    pub detect_mixed_indentation: bool,\n202\t    /// Whether to check for consistent indentation (multiples of base)\n203\t    pub check_consistent_indentation: bool,\n204\t    /// Base indentation size (usually 2 or 4)\n205\t    pub base_indent_size: usize,\n206\t    /// Whether to validate delimiter balancing\n207\t    pub validate_delimiter_balance: bool,\n208\t    /// Whether to detect duplicate keys\n209\t    pub detect_duplicate_keys: bool,\n210\t    /// Whether to detect invalid sequence syntax\n211\t    pub detect_invalid_sequences: bool,\n212\t    /// Whether to detect invalid mapping syntax\n213\t    pub detect_invalid_mappings: bool,\n214\t}\n215\t\n216\timpl Default for DetectorConfig {\n217\t    fn default() -> Self {\n218\t        Self {\n219\t            detect_mixed_indentation: true,\n220\t            check_consistent_indentation: true,\n221\t            base_indent_size: 2,\n222\t            validate_delimiter_balance: true,\n223\t            detect_duplicate_keys: true,\n224\t            detect_invalid_sequences: true,\n225\t            detect_invalid_mappings: true,\n226\t        }\n227\t    }\n228\t}\n229\t\n230\t/// State tracking for indentation analysis\n231\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\n232\tstruct IndentationState {\n233\t    /// Whether tabs have been seen in the file\n234\t    has_tabs: bool,\n235\t    /// Whether spaces have been seen in the file\n236\t    has_spaces: bool,\n237\t    /// Previous line's indentation level (in spaces)\n238\t    prev_indent_level: usize,\n239\t    /// Expected indentation levels for nested contexts\n240\t    indent_stack: Vec<usize>,\n241\t    /// Lines with mixed indentation\n242\t    mixed_lines: Vec<usize>,\n243\t}\n244\t\n245\t/// State tracking for delimiter analysis\n246\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\n247\tstruct DelimiterState {\n248\t    /// Stack of opening brackets/braces\n249\t    bracket_stack: Vec<(char, usize)>, // (bracket_type, Whether we're inside a multiline block (|, >)\n255\t    in_multiline_block: bool,\n256\t    /// Indentation level of the multiline block start\n257\t    multiline_block_indent: usize,\n258\t    /// Whether we're inside flow-style context (within [] or {})\n259\t    in_flow_context: bool,\n260\t}\n261\t\n262\t/// Quote type for tracking\n263\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\n264\tenum QuoteType {\n265\t    Single,\n266\t    Double,\n267\t}\n268\t\n269\t/// State tracking for structure analysis\n270\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\n271\tstruct StructureState {\n272\t    /// Stack of nested structures (mapping, sequence, etc.)\n273\t    context_stack: Vec<StructureContext>,\n274\t    /// Hierarchical scope-based key tracking\n275\t    scope_stack: ScopeScopeStack,\n276\t    /// Previous line's indentation level\n277\t    prev_indent: usize,\n278\t}\n279\t\n280\t/// Current structure context\n281\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\n282\tenum StructureContext {\n283\t    Mapping,\n284\t    Sequence,\n285\t    FlowMapping,\n286\t    FlowSequence,\n287\t}\n288\t\n289\timpl SyntaxDetector {\n290\t    /// Create a new syntax detector with default configuration\n291\t    pub fn new() -> Self {\n292\t        Self::with_config(DetectorConfig::default())\n293\t    }\n294\t\n295\t    /// Create a new syntax detector with custom configuration\n296\t    pub fn with_config(config: DetectorConfig) -> Self {\n297\t        Self {\n298\t            config,\n299\t            indentation_state: IndentationState::default(),\n300\t            delimiter_state: DelimiterState::default(),\n301\t            structure_state: StructureState::default(),\n302\t        }\n303\t    }\n304\t\n305\t    /// Detect all syntax errors in YAML content\n306\t    ///\n307\t    /// This is the main entry point for syntax detection. It runs all\n308\t    /// enabled detectors and returns a comprehensive list of errors.\n309\t    ///\n310\t    /// # Arguments\n311\t    /// * `content` - The YAML content to analyze\n312\t    ///\n313\t    /// # Returns\n314\t    /// A vector of validation errors representing all detected syntax issues\n315\t    pub fn detect_errors(&mut self, content: &str) -> Vec<ValidationError> {\n316\t        let mut errors = Vec::new();\n317\t        let lines: Vec<&str> = content.lines().collect();\n318\t\n319\t        // Reset state for new detection\n320\t        self.reset_state();\n321\t\n322\t        for (line_num, line) in lines.iter().enumerate() {\n323\t            let line_num_1index = line_num + 1; // Convert to 1-indexed\n324\t\n325\t            // Skip empty lines and comments for most checks\n326\t            let is_meaningful = !line.trim().is_empty() && !line.trim().starts_with('#');\n327\t\n328\t            if is_meaningful {\n329\t                // Run all enabled detectors\n330\t                if self.config.detect_mixed_indentation || self.config.check_consistent_indentation {\n331\t                    self.detect_indentation_errors(line, line_num_1index, &mut errors);\n332\t                }\n333\t\n334\t                if self.config.validate_delimiter_balance {\n335\t                    self.detect_delimiter_errors(line, line_num_1index, &mut errors);\n336\t                }\n337\t\n338\t                if self.config.detect_invalid_sequences || self.config.detect_invalid_mappings {\n339\t                    self.detect_structure_errors(line, line_num_1index, &mut errors);\n340\t                }\n341\t\n342\t                if self.config.detect_duplicate_keys {\n343\t                    self.detect_duplicate_key_errors(line, line_num_1index, &mut errors);\n344\t                }\n345\t            }\n346\t        }\n347\t\n348\t        // Final validation checks\n349\t        self.finalize_delimiter_checks(&mut errors);\n350\t        self.finalize_structure_checks(&mut errors);\n351\t\n352\t        errors\n353\t    }\n354\t\n355\t    /// Detect syntax errors and convert to ValidationResult\n356\t    pub fn detect_to_validation_result(&mut self, content: &str) -> ValidationResult {\n357\t        let errors = self.detect_errors(content);\n358\t\n359\t        if errors.is_empty() {\n360\t            ValidationResult::success()\n361\t        } else {\n362\t            ValidationResult::failure(errors)\n363\t        }\n364\t    }\n365\t\n366\t    /// Reset all state tracking for a new detection run\n367\t    fn reset_state(&mut self) {\n368\t        self.indentation_state = IndentationState::default();\n369\t        self.delimiter_state = DelimiterState::default();\n370\t        self.structure_state = StructureState::default();\n371\t    }\n372\t\n373\t    /// Detect indentation-related errors\n374\t    fn detect_indentation_errors(&mut self, line: &str, line_num: usize, errors: &mut Vec<ValidationError>) {\n375\t        let leading_whitespace_len = self.get_leading_whitespace_length(line);\n376\t\n377\t        // Check for mixed tabs and spaces in leading whitespace\n378\t        if self.config.detect_mixed_indentation && leading_whitespace_len > 0 {\n379\t            let leading_chars: Vec<char> = line.chars().take(leading_whitespace_len).collect();\n380\t            let has_tabs = leading_chars.contains(&'\\t');\n381\t            let has_spaces = leading_chars.contains(&' ');\n382\t\n383\t            if has_tabs && has_spaces {\n384\t                self.indentation_state.has_tabs = true;\n385\t                self.indentation_state.has_spaces = true;\n386\t                self.indentation_state.mixed_lines.push(line_num);\n387\t\n388\t                let error_type = IndentationErrorType::MixedTabsAndSpaces;\n389\t                errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n390\t                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n391\t                    error_type.description()\n392\t                ).with_line(line_num).with_indentation_error_type(error_type));\n393\t            } else if has_tabs {\n394\t                self.indentation_state.has_tabs = true;\n395\t                // Optionally report tab-only indentation\n396\t                if self.config.detect_mixed_indentation {\n397\t                    let error_type = IndentationErrorType::TabCharacter;\n398\t                    errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n399\t                        format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n400\t                        format!(\"{}: {}\", error_type.code(), error_type.description())\n401\t                    ).with_line(line_num).with_indentation_error_type(error_type));\n402\t                }\n403\t            } else if has_spaces {\n404\t                self.indentation_state.has_spaces = true;\n405\t            }\n406\t        }\n407\t\n408\t        // Check for consistent indentation levels\n409\t        if self.config.check_consistent_indentation && leading_whitespace_len > 0 {\n410\t            let indent_level = leading_whitespace_len;\n411\t\n412\t            // Check if indentation is a multiple of base_indent_size\n413\t            if indent_level % self.config.base_indent_size != 0 {\n414\t                let error_type = IndentationErrorType::InvalidIndentLevel;\n415\t                errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n416\t                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n417\t                    format!(\"{}: {} spaces is not a multiple of {}\",\n418\t                            error_type.code(), indent_level, self.config.base_indent_size)\n419\t                ).with_line(line_num).with_indentation_error_type(error_type));\n420\t            }\n421\t\n422\t            // Check if indentation increase is consistent\n423\t            if indent_level > self.indentation_state.prev_indent_level {\n424\t                let increase = indent_level - self.indentation_state.prev_indent_level;\n425\t\n426\t                // Check if increase is too large (more than 2 levels at once)\n427\t                if increase > self.config.base_indent_size * 2 {\n428\t                    let error_type = IndentationErrorType::ExcessiveIndentIncrease;\n429\t                    errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n430\t                        format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n431\t                        format!(\"{}: increase of {} spaces exceeds maximum of {} levels\",\n432\t                                error_type.code(), increase, self.config.base_indent_size * 2)\n433\t                    ).with_line(line_num).with_indentation_error_type(error_type));\n434\t                }\n435\t\n436\t                if increase % self.config.base_indent_size != 0 {\n437\t                    let error_type = IndentationErrorType::InvalidIndentIncrease;\n438\t                    errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n439\t                        format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n440\t                        format!(\"{}: increase of {} spaces is not a multiple of {}\",\n441\t                                error_type.code(), increase, self.config.base_indent_size)\n442\t                    ).with_line(line_num).with_indentation_error_type(error_type));\n443\t                } else {\n444\t                    // Valid increase - push to stack\n445\t                    self.indentation_state.indent_stack.push(indent_level);\n446\t                }\n447\t            } else if indent_level < self.indentation_state.prev_indent_level {\n448\t                // Decrease in indentation - pop from stack\n449\t                while !self.indentation_state.indent_stack.is_empty()\n450\t                      && *self.indentation_state.indent_stack.last().unwrap() > indent_level {\n451\t                    self.indentation_state.indent_stack.pop();\n452\t                }\n453\t            }\n454\t\n455\t            self.indentation_state.prev_indent_level = indent_level;\n456\t        }\n457\t    }\n458\t\n459\t    /// Detect delimiter-related errors\n460\t    fn detect_delimiter_errors(&mut self, line: &str, line_num: usize, errors: &mut Vec<ValidationError>) {\n461\t        let leading_whitespace_len = self.get_leading_whitespace_length(line);\n462\t        let trimmed = line.trim();\n463\t\n464\t        // Check if we're inside a multiline block\n465\t        if self.delimiter_state.in_multiline_block {\n466\t            // Multiline blocks continue until we find a line with less or equal indentation\n467\t            // that's not empty or a comment\n468\t            if leading_whitespace_len > self.delimiter_state.multiline_block_indent ||\n469\t               (leading_whitespace_len == self.delimiter_state.multiline_block_indent && (trimmed.is_empty() || trimmed.starts_with('#'))) {\n470\t                return; // Skip lines inside the multiline block\n471\t            } else {\n472\t                // Exit multiline block when indentation decreases\n473\t                self.delimiter_state.in_multiline_block = false;\n474\t                self.delimiter_state.multiline_block_indent = 0;\n475\t            }\n476\t        }\n477\t\n478\t        // Skip empty lines and comments (but not when inside multiline blocks - handled above)\n479\t        if trimmed.is_empty() || trimmed.starts_with('#') {\n480\t            return;\n481\t        }\n482\t\n483\t        // Handle document markers (---, ...)\n484\t        if trimmed == \"---\" || trimmed == \"...\" {\n485\t            return; // Document markers should not be flagged for missing colons\n486\t        }\n487\t\n488\t        // Handle multiline block scalars (|, >, |-, |- , >-, >+, |+)\n489\t        // These appear after a colon, like \"key: |\" or \"key: >\"\n490\t        if trimmed.contains(\": |\") || trimmed.contains(\": >\") ||\n491\t           trimmed.contains(\":|-\") || trimmed.contains(\":>-\") ||\n492\t           trimmed.contains(\":|+\") || trimmed.contains(\":>+\") {\n493\t            self.delimiter_state.in_multiline_block = true;\n494\t            self.delimiter_state.multiline_block_indent = leading_whitespace_len; // Content must be MORE indented than this line\n495\t        }\n496\t\n497\t        // Check for missing colons after keys\n498\t        if !trimmed.starts_with('-') && // Not a sequence item\n499\t           !trimmed.starts_with('?') && // Not an explicit key\n500\t           !trimmed.starts_with(':') && // Not a value\n501\t           !trimmed.starts_with('&') && // Not an anchor\n502\t           !trimmed.starts_with('*') && // Not an alias\n503\t           !trimmed.starts_with('!') && // Not a tag\n504\t           !trimmed.starts_with('|') && // Not a literal block scalar\n505\t           !trimmed.starts_with('>') && // Not a folded block scalar\n506\t           !trimmed.contains('{') && // Not flow style mapping\n507\t           !trimmed.contains('[') && // Not flow style sequence\n508\t           !trimmed.contains(':') && // No colon at all\n509\t           !trimmed.is_empty() &&\n510\t           self.looks_like_key(trimmed) {\n511\t\n512\t            let error_type = DelimiterErrorType::MissingColon;\n513\t            errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n514\t                format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n515\t                format!(\"{}: {}\", error_type.code(), error_type.description())\n516\t            ).with_line(line_num).with_delimiter_error_type(error_type));\n517\t        }\n518\t\n519\t        // Track bracket/brace balance and flow context\n520\t        for (char_pos, ch) in line.chars().enumerate() {\n521\t            match ch {\n522\t                '[' => {\n523\t                    self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack.push(('[', line_num));\n524\t                    self.delimiter_state.in_flow_context = true;\n525\t                }\n526\t                ']' => {\n527\t                    if let Some(('[', _)) = self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack.last() {\n528\t                        self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack.pop();\n529\t                        // Update flow context when stack becomes empty\n530\t                        self.delimiter_state.in_flow_context = !self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack.is_empty();\n531\t                    } else {\n532\t                        let error_type = DelimiterErrorType::UnmatchedClosingBracket;\n533\t                        errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n534\t                            format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n535\t                            format!(\"{}: {}\", error_type.code(), error_type.description())\n536\t                        ).with_line(line_num).with_delimiter_error_type(error_type));\n537\t                    }\n538\t                }\n539\t                '{' => {\n540\t                    self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack.push(('{', line_num));\n541\t                    self.delimiter_state.in_flow_context = true;\n542\t                }\n543\t                '}' => {\n544\t                    if let Some(('{', _)) = self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack.last() {\n545\t                        self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack.pop();\n546\t                        // Update flow context when stack becomes empty\n547\t                        self.delimiter_state.in_flow_context = !self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack.is_empty();\n548\t                    } else {\n549\t                        let error_type = DelimiterErrorType::UnmatchedClosingBrace;\n550\t                        errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n551\t                            format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n552\t                            format!(\"{}: {}\", error_type.code(), error_type.description())\n553\t                        ).with_line(line_num).with_delimiter_error_type(error_type));\n554\t                    }\n555\t                }\n556\t                '\\'' => {\n557\t                    match self.delimiter_state.quote_state {\n558\t                        None => self.delimiter_state.quote_state = Some(QuoteType::Single),\n559\t                        Some(QuoteType::Single) => self.delimiter_state.quote_state = None,\n560\t                        Some(QuoteType::Double) => {\n561\t                            // Mismatched quote\n562\t                            let error_type = DelimiterErrorType::MismatchedQuotes;\n563\t                            errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n564\t                                format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n565\t                                format!(\"{}: {} (single inside double)\", error_type.code(), error_type.description())\n566\t                            ).with_line(line_num).with_delimiter_error_type(error_type));\n567\t                            self.delimiter_state.quote_errors.push(line_num);\n568\t                        }\n569\t                    }\n570\t                }\n571\t                '\"' => {\n572\t                    match self.delimiter_state.quote_state {\n573\t                        None => self.delimiter_state.quote_state = Some(QuoteType::Double),\n574\t                        Some(QuoteType::Double) => self.delimiter_state.quote_state = None,\n575\t                        Some(QuoteType::Single) => {\n576\t                            // Mismatched quote\n577\t                            let error_type = DelimiterErrorType::MismatchedQuotes;\n578\t                            errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n579\t                                format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n580\t                                format!(\"{}: {} (double inside single)\", error_type.code(), error_type.description())\n581\t                            ).with_line(line_num).with_delimiter_error_type(error_type));\n582\t                            self.delimiter_state.quote_errors.push(line_num);\n583\t                        }\n584\t                    }\n585\t                }\n586\t                _ => {}\n587\t            }\n588\t        }\n589\t    }\n590\t\n591\t    /// Detect structure-related errors\n592\t    fn detect_structure_errors(&mut self, line: &str, line_num: usize, errors: &mut Vec<ValidationError>) {\n593\t        let trimmed = line.trim();\n594\t\n595\t        // Detect invalid sequence syntax\n596\t        if self.config.detect_invalid_sequences {\n597\t            if trimmed.starts_with(\"- \") {\n598\t                // Valid sequence item\n599\t                if self.structure_state.context_stack.is_empty() ||\n600\t                   !matches!(self.structure_state.context_stack.last(), Some(StructureContext::Sequence)) {\n601\t                    self.structure_state.context_stack.push(StructureContext::Sequence);\n602\t                }\n603\t            } else if trimmed.starts_with('-') && trimmed.len() > 1 {\n604\t                // Dash followed by something other than space\n605\t                if let Some(next_char) = trimmed.chars().nth(1) {\n606\t                    if !next_char.is_whitespace() {\n607\t                        errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n608\t                            format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n609\t                            \"sequence dash '-' must be followed by whitespace\"\n610\t                        ).with_line(line_num));\n611\t                    }\n612\t                }\n613\t            }\n614\t        }\n615\t\n616\t        // Detect invalid mapping syntax\n617\t        if self.config.detect_invalid_mappings {\n618\t            if let Some(colon_pos) = trimmed.find(':') {\n619\t                let before_colon = &trimmed[..colon_pos];\n620\t\n621\t                // Check if colon is properly used after a key\n622\t                if !before_colon.is_empty() && !before_colon.ends_with(' ') {\n623\t                    // Valid key: value pair\n624\t                    if self.structure_state.context_stack.is_empty() ||\n625\t                       !matches!(self.structure_state.context_stack.last(), Some(StructureContext::Mapping)) {\n626\t                        self.structure_state.context_stack.push(StructureContext::Mapping);\n627\t                    }\n628\t                } else if before_colon.is_empty() {\n629\t                    // Colon at start - might be a value or error\n630\t                    if !trimmed.starts_with(\": \") && trimmed != \":\" {\n631\t                        errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n632\t                            format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n633\t                            \"colon ':' at start must be followed by space\"\n634\t                        ).with_line(line_num));\n635\t                    }\n636\t                }\n637\t            }\n638\t        }\n639\t\n640\t        // Check for flow syntax indicators\n641\t        if trimmed.contains('{') || trimmed.contains('[') {\n642\t            self.structure_state.context_stack.push(if trimmed.contains('{') {\n643\t                StructureContext::FlowMapping\n644\t            } else {\n645\t                StructureContext::FlowSequence\n646\t            });\n647\t        }\n648\t    }\n649\t\n650\t    /// Detect duplicate key errors using scope-aware tracking\n651\t    fn detect_duplicate_key_errors(&mut self, line: &str, line_num: usize, errors: &mut Vec<ValidationError>) {\n652\t        // Skip duplicate key detection when inside flow-style contexts ([] or {})\n653\t        // Flow-style YAML uses {key: value} syntax which should not be treated as duplicate keys\n654\t        if self.delimiter_state.in_flow_context {\n655\t            return;\n656\t        }\n657\t\n658\t        let trimmed = line.trim();\n659\t        let indent = self.get_leading_whitespace_length(line);\n660\t\n661\t        // Check if this is a sequence item with a key-value pair (e.g., \"- key: value\")\n662\t        // This creates a new scope for each sequence item\n663\t        if trimmed.starts_with(\"- \") {\n664\t            if let Some(colon_pos) = trimmed.find(':') {\n665\t                // We have \"- key: value\" format\n666\t                let key_part = &trimmed[2..colon_pos]; // Skip \"- \" to get the key\n667\t                let key = key_part.trim();\n668\t\n669\t                // Only enter sequence scope if it looks like a valid mapping key\n670\t                if !key.is_empty() && !key.starts_with('\\'') && !key.starts_with('\"')\n671\t                    && !key.contains('#') && !key.contains('?') {\n672\t                    self.structure_state.scope_stack.enter_sequence_scope(indent, line_num);\n673\t                }\n674\t            }\n675\t            // Sequence items don't need duplicate checking at this level\n676\t            // The keys within the sequence item will be checked when we process them\n677\t            return;\n678\t        }\n679\t\n680\t        // Extract key if this is a key-value pair\n681\t        if let Some(colon_pos) = trimmed.find(':') {\n682\t            let key_part = &trimmed[..colon_pos];\n683\t\n684\t            // Check if this is a parent key (ends with colon and no value on same line)\n685\t            // A parent key typically has nothing after the colon or just whitespace/comments\n686\t            let after_colon = trimmed[colon_pos + 1..].trim();\n687\t            let is_parent_key = after_colon.is_empty() ||\n688\t                                after_colon.starts_with('#');\n689\t\n690\t            if is_parent_key {\n691\t                // This is a parent key - extract the key name and enter a new scope\n692\t                if !key_part.is_empty() && !key_part.contains('-') && !key_part.contains('?') {\n693\t                    let key = key_part.trim();\n694\t                    if !key.starts_with('\\'') && !key.starts_with('\"') && !key.contains('#') {\n695\t                        // Enter a new scope for the parent key's nested content\n696\t                        self.structure_state.scope_stack.enter_scope(\n697\t                            indent + self.config.base_indent_size,\n698\t                            line_num,\n699\t                            Some(key.to_string())\n700\t                        );\n701\t                    }\n702\t                }\n703\t                return;\n704\t            }\n705\t\n706\t            // Skip if it's a value or not a simple key\n707\t            if key_part.is_empty() || key_part.contains('-') || key_part.contains('?') {\n708\t                return;\n709\t            }\n710\t\n711\t            let key = key_part.trim();\n712\t\n713\t            // Skip quoted keys and special keys\n714\t            if key.starts_with('\\'') || key.starts_with('\"') || key.contains('#') {\n715\t                return;\n716\t            }\n717\t\n718\t            // Handle scope transitions based on indentation changes\n719\t            use std::cmp::Ordering;\n720\t            match indent.cmp(&self.structure_state.scope_stack.current_indent()) {\n721\t                Ordering::Greater => {\n722\t                    // Indent increased - this should be a parent key (handled above)\n723\t                    // If we get here, it's a regular key at deeper indent without a parent\n724\t                    // Enter an anonymous scope\n725\t                    self.structure_state.scope_stack.enter_scope(indent, line_num, None);\n726\t                }\n727\t                Ordering::Less => {\n728\t                    // Indent decreased - exit to the appropriate parent scope\n729\t                    self.structure_state.scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n730\t                }\n731\t                Ordering::Equal => {\n732\t                    // Same scope level - continue checking for duplicates\n733\t                }\n734\t            }\n735\t\n736\t            // Check for duplicates in the current scope\n737\t            if self.structure_state.scope_stack.contains_key(key) {\n738\t                let scope_path = self.structure_state.scope_stack.get_scope_path();\n739\t                let error_message = if scope_path.is_empty() {\n740\t                    format!(\"duplicate key '{}' in mapping scope\", key)\n741\t                } else {\n742\t                    format!(\"duplicate key '{}' in mapping scope '{}'\", key, scope_path)\n743\t                };\n744\t                errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n745\t                    format!(\"key_{}\", key),\n746\t                    error_message\n747\t                ).with_line(line_num));\n748\t            } else {\n749\t                self.structure_state.scope_stack.add_key(key);\n750\t            }\n751\t        }\n752\t\n753\t        // Update previous indentation for next comparison\n754\t        self.structure_state.prev_indent = indent;\n755\t    }\n756\t\n757\t    /// Finalize delimiter checks after processing all lines\n758\t    fn finalize_delimiter_checks(&mut self, errors: &mut Vec<ValidationError>) {\n759\t        // Check for unclosed brackets\n760\t        for (bracket, line_num) in &self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack {\n761\t            let error_type = match *bracket {\n762\t                '[' => DelimiterErrorType::UnclosedBracket,\n763\t                '{' => DelimiterErrorType::UnclosedBrace,\n764\t                _ => continue, // Skip unknown bracket types\n765\t            };\n766\t            errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n767\t                \"delimiter_balance\".to_string(),\n768\t                format!(\"{}: {}\", error_type.code(), error_type.description())\n769\t            ).with_line(*line_num).with_delimiter_error_type(error_type));\n770\t        }\n771\t\n772\t        // Check for unclosed quotes\n773\t        if self.delimiter_state.quote_state.is_some() {\n774\t            let (error_type, quote_char) = match self.delimiter_state.quote_state {\n775\t                Some(QuoteType::Single) => (DelimiterErrorType::UnclosedSingleQuote, \"'\"),\n776\t                Some(QuoteType::Double) => (DelimiterErrorType::UnclosedDoubleQuote, \"\\\"\"),\n777\t                None => unreachable!(),\n778\t            };\n779\t\n780\t            errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n781\t                \"quote_balance\".to_string(),\n782\t                format!(\"{}: {}\", error_type.code(), error_type.description())\n783\t            ).with_line(0).with_delimiter_error_type(error_type)); // Line 0 since we don't track where it opened\n784\t        }\n785\t    }\n786\t\n787\t    /// Finalize structure checks after processing all lines\n788\t    fn finalize_structure_checks(&mut self, _errors: &mut Vec<ValidationError>) {\n789\t        // No-op - same-level duplicate detection is handled in detect_duplicate_key_errors\n790\t    }\n791\t\n792\t    /// Get leading whitespace length from a line\n793\t    fn get_leading_whitespace_length(&self, line: &str) -> usize {\n794\t        line.chars().take_while(|c| c.is_whitespace()).count()\n795\t    }\n796\t\n797\t    /// Check if a line looks like a key definition\n798\t    fn looks_like_key(&self, trimmed: &str) -> bool {\n799\t        // Keys are typically alphanumeric with underscores, dashes, or dots\n800\t        // They don't start with special YAML characters\n801\t        if trimmed.is_empty() {\n802\t            return false;\n803\t        }\n804\t\n805\t        let first_char = trimmed.chars().next().unwrap();\n806\t\n807\t        // Exclude lines that start with YAML special characters\n808\t        if matches!(first_char, '-' | ':' | '?' | '|' | '>' | '#' | '[' | ']' | '{' | '}' | '\"' | '\\'' | '!' | '&' | '*' | '%' | '@' | '`') {\n809\t            return false;\n810\t        }\n811\t\n812\t        // Look for word characters at the start\n813\t        trimmed.chars().next().map_or(false, |c| c.is_alphanumeric() || c == '_' || c == '.')\n814\t    }\n815\t}\n816\t\n817\timpl Default for SyntaxDetector {\n818\t    fn default() -> Self {\n819\t        Self::new()\n820\t    }\n821\t}\n822\t\n823\t#[cfg(test)]\n824\tmod tests {\n825\t    use super::*;\n826\t\n827\t    #[test]\n828\t    fn test_detect_mixed_indentation() {\n829\t        let mut detector = SyntaxDetector::new();\n830\t        let yaml = \"key: value\\n\\t  bad: value\";\n831\t        let errors = detector.detect_errors(yaml);\n832\t\n833\t        assert!(!errors.is_empty());\n834\t        assert!(errors.iter().any(|e| e.message.contains(\"mixed tabs and spaces\")));\n835\t    }\n836\t\n837\t    #[test]\n838\t    fn test_detect_missing_colon() {\n839\t        let mut detector = SyntaxDetector::new();\n840\t        let yaml = \"key value\\n  key2: value2\";\n841\t        let errors = detector.detect_errors(yaml);\n842\t\n843\t        assert!(!errors.is_empty());\n844\t        assert!(errors.iter().any(|e| e.message.contains(\"missing colon\")));\n845\t    }\n846\t\n847\t    #[test]\n848\t    fn test_detect_unmatched_bracket() {\n849\t        let mut detector = SyntaxDetector::new();\n850\t        let yaml = \"key: [value1, value2\";\n851\t        let errors = detector.detect_errors(yaml);\n852\t\n853\t
